801: Verditius first apprentice, Gelon, becomes Primus of the House, though he lived at Val-Negra for decades. 

*Gelon is renowned for his many enchanted swords and his followers established the Confraternity of Roland.*

801 Rhine: Quendalon of Merinita exits faerie woods with ruby eyes, and preaches a new, faerie way of magic, which he claims would revolutionize magical theory. Primus Myanar refuses to acknowledge him and claims that Quendalon is actually a changeling, and mobilizes his faction to resist. An internal House struggle ensues, resulting in the death of Myanar and the loss of Fenistal.

*The debate over whether this actually was Quendalon continues to this day.*

802 Levant: With the caliph's permission, Urbs Rubra covenant is founded at Petra in southern Palestine.

802 Rhine: Quendalon retakes control of House Merinita and converts it to his vision. Irencillia becomes the new domus magna of the house, and it becomes dominated by faerie magic. The new elder Elder Gild (Holundergilde) splits off from the Hawthorn Gild to pursue the fae-focused agenda. While they still believe in the sanctity of the wilderness, they concentrate solely on the preservation of the fae and promoting friendly contact between magi and faeries.

*Myanars followers are declared orbi of Merinita and are eventually adopted by House Bjornaer, where the line dwindles and vanishes.*

802 Rome: Verditius’s filius Milo discovers one of the treasures of Hephaestus,

the mechanized torture chair used by Zeus to interrogate his wife Hera. Returning it to Verdi, Milo and Verditius’s first filius, Gelon, quarrel over who should possess the chair. Verditius decides that ownership would be awarded to the filius who could invent the most wondrous magical item, giving them a year to complete the task.

803–807 Rhine: Covenant Irencillia comes under frequent attacks. \
*Some say that Myanar, transformed into a snake, led these attacks, and that faeries participated in the defense of the covenant. \
Some speculate that Myanar was distantly related to Tytalus the Founder, and had asked for his help against Quendalon - the reason for his presence there in 807 when he disappeared.*

803 Rome: The first Verdi Competition. Verditius summons all the Verditius magi in the Order to judge the event. Gelon wins the contest, and benevolently gives Hephaestus torture chair to Verdi rather than accepting the item himself. This is such a well received event that Verditius decides to hold it again, eighteen years later, allowing all of his line to participate.

*This tradition has lasted throughout the centuries; even the Schism War did not*

*interrupt the Contest of 1018.*

803: Flavia filia Jerbiton, vanishes from the Order.

803 The Spider War: Pralix filia Tytalus arrives at Wearmouth in the Kingdom of Northumbria with seven magi and a half-hundred of grogs. At the time, the Kingdom of Northumbria covered most of southern Scotland and northern England, and was believed to be the seat of Damhan-Allaidh’s power.

804 The Spider War: Pralix’s Hermetic army first clashes with Damhan-Allaidh’s forces in the Massacre of Bowland Forest. It’s an utter disaster for the Order, resulting in the death of all five of the junior magi, leaving just Pralix and Ignes Nefastus of House Flambeau still alive. The two magi retreat south and are offered succor by the native swynwyr, experts in warding magics, in their impregnable stronghold in North Wales that had resisted Damhan-Allaidh for many decades.

*Using the swynwyr’s island fortress as a base, Pralix spent three years gathering together wizards from the British Isles who, like the swynwyr, had suffered the tyranny of Damhan-Allaidh and his allies.*

806 The Spider War: Pralix meets with Diedne on the shores of Loch Cuan in Ulster, Hibernia, where she is in order to gain recruits to the cause against Damhan-Allaidh. Hearing of her presence Diedne made haste from Ailech to meet with her. Diedne believed that Pralix had come to assist in her pogrom against the druids; Pralix thought she could convince Diedne to allow her to enlist the druids without forcing them to join the Order. Diedne refuses to allow her to enlist Irish magicians; she is insistent that any druids must join the Order of Hermes or else die. Diedne is further incensed when Pralix laughs at her and calls her stupid and petty. Neither side could see reason, and the two magae famously quarreled, their argument escalating into an illegal Wizard’s War. Both survive the encounter (although the local monastery does not). Despite this encounter at Loch Cuan, Pralix still returns to Britain with a cadre of Irish druids prepared to oppose their Scottish counterparts. Neither maga brought charges against the other, and it was never revealed who struck first. 

*Or who fled the fight…*

*Neither brought charges against the other at Tribunal, but it is notable that after the war with Damhan-Allaidh Diedne was one of the most vocal detractors of Pralix and insisted that she be Marched for her crimes. *

806 Levant: With the caliph's permission, the Urania covenant is founded in the Caucasus.

807 The Spider War: Pralix decides her army is ready, and tracks down Damhan-Allaidh using shape-shifting scouts and flying witches. They meet in battle on the Roman Wall, at the abandoned fort Hunnum. Rather than being alone, Damhan-Allaidh is accompanied by several magicians, a force of Northumbrian soldiers, and two giants. Seemingly warned of Pralix’s approach. The Battle of Hunnum ends in another defeat for Pralix, but this time her retreat is orderly and casualties manageable. She withdraws to a camp to the north called Loch Leglean, and regroups.

*Damhadh-Duidas venture that their opponent had seers at his command who can see the future; Ignes Nefastus considers a spy amongst them to be the more likely option, and his aspersion on the loyalty of Damhadh-Duidas is not missed. \
*Pralix proposes her council to teach the Gifted members of their army the Parma Magica; as the only way to gain an advantage over the enemy. Ignes Nefastus vehemently disagrees, and accuses her of wanting to betray the Order’s secrets. The row nearly comes to blows, although Giaro of Mercere manage to avert actual violence. Ignes Nefastus storms out from the Loch Leglean camp, vowing to have nothing more to do with Pralix, and walks straight into Damhan-Allaidh himself. The sorcerer had been spying on Loch Leglean, and an immense magical battle ensues. The hills tremble with the power of their magic. Ignes Nefastus 

flings flames at his opponent, only to have them turn back upon himself. None 

of his spells seem to get any purchase upon The Spider, but likewise the Flambeau magus’s Parma Magica protects him from the worst of his opponent’s 

spells. This duel later becomes known as the Battle of the Wounded Glen because of the damage inflicted on the local environment. It ends with the death of Ignes Nefastus, his mutilated body thrown over the walls of Loch Leglean. 

*Spectators noticed that Damhan-Allaidh fled the battlefield sorely wounded. \
With Ignes Nefastus gone, Damhadh-Duidas became Pralix’s chief adviser. They *

*proceeded with the plan to share the Parma Magica and drilled the hedge magicians in its use. Each winter Pralix retreated to Wales to plan and in the summer she campaigned in the kingdom of Northumbria using Loch Leglean as her base. *

807: Founder Tytalus, one of the most feared wizards of his time, leaves Fudarus dressed only in a leper’s robe and veil, his familiar nowhere to be seen. He visits every member of his House one by one, offering advice to each. Then he enters Maddenhofen Woods to challenge the Queen of Faerie "for his heart’s desire", and is not seen thereafter. His favored filia Hariste moves to Fudarus and becomes the first Prima of his House. 

*Allegedly, his last port of call was to Hariste, and he tarried with her for over a month. Pralix never fully accepted Hariste. To her, Tytalus was a harsh but beloved father figure, which cast Hariste in the role of a gold-digging strumpet, and one nearly half her own age.*

808 The Spider War: A group of Pralix’s magicians sailing into the estuary of the River Humber encounter a blockade across the river at the small port of Goole. It’s manned by the rune-wizard Centwine, who single-handedly manage to hold them up for three days, harrying them with his magic before he’s joined by his allies and the boat is boarded and the occupants killed.

*The ship was somehow stuck onto the blockade, unable to retreat or break through. The sea swelled violently and landing boats were swept away. The ship’s inhabitants were struck with a raging thirst, and the river water retreated from their lowered buckets. All of their food turned to maggots and beetles. Even the locals of Goole joined in by hurling flaming rags and sticky tar at the beleaguered ship.*

Later in September, an elementalist of Damhan-Allaidh raise the Grey Man of the Merrick, the genius loci of the mountain range known as The Awful Hand (because of its five spurs) in Galloway. He lies in ambush for Pralix and her warband, but the ambush is spotted and battle joined. The tide turns in Pralix’s favor when the mountain spirit realizes it’s being used as a weapon in a petty human battle, and it abandons the field of war.

C. 808–810 Normandy: The new Prima Hariste of Tytalus collates the Founder’s wisdom, by personally visiting all House members sans Pralix who refuses to see her, into the "Analects of Tytalus". 

*Later on her deathbed, her last instruction is that the book should be copied and given to every member of the House. To this day, every apprentice copies his*

*master’s work for himself at some point during his apprenticeship.*

809 Levant: Caliph Harun dies and civil war breaks out between his sons. The

attention of the Muslim world turns inward, and contact with the Hermetic covenants is brought to an abrupt end. The covenants find themselves forgotten by their former Muslim comrades, a state further reinforced by the fragmentation of the Muslim world.

*The Tribunal continued to be a quiet backwater of the Order until the period of The Crusades.*

809 The Spider War: Pralix fights the war on two fronts, with herself in command in the south and Damhadh-Duidas commanding in the north. \
In May, Giaro of Mercere is running messages between the two forces when he comes across an expeditionary force of druids and giants quite by chance in Redesdale. They are clearly trying to outflank Pralix’s forces by taking a shortcut. Realizing that he has no time to warn Pralix, Giaro goes amongst the wild border clans of Redesdale, famous for their banditry and lawlessness. He convinces them through subterfuge and bravado to stage an ambush against the interlopers and successfully drives them back, killing two giants in the process. *Redesdale men still find favor in the turbs of magi who know their history.*

On All Hallow’s Eve, Pralix’s forces are delayed in Loch Leglean by bad weather. Snow is blocking all passes through the Southern Uplands. As the moon sets, an unearthly howl is heard and a truly dreadful host falls upon Loch Leglean with the sluagh, a vast horde of angry ghosts raised by dark magics from the kingdom of Sluag (the Pictish god of death). They swarm over the camp, killing indiscriminately. Less than a dozen magicians are killed that night, but it’s still the greatest single loss suffered by Pralix’s army since she taught them the Parma Magica. \
*Many more are left insane, gibbering at the horrors they witnessed and unable to sleep for the rest of their lives. *

810 Iberia: Somnus and Atropos ex Tremere found the covenant of Secunda with the remnants of their House in the Peninsula. This covenant acts as a base of operations for the Tremere in Iberia.

Tello, Ranemiro's former apprentice, establishes the covenant of Alma Mater.

810 Normandy: Confluensis covenant founded at the lair of the dragon Gargouille in the swamps that lie between Rouen and the mouth of the Seine. Under the instruction of Founder Guernicus to ensure that Hermetic Law is respected at the edges of the Order.

*Throughout its history, the covenant has served the policies of the Primus of House Guernicus. During the many crises of the Order, its current location has served as a mustering point and logistical base for the House.*

810: Founder Jerbiton mentions his belief that Flavia, his filia, has likely died. *She had not. See 819.*

810 The Spider War: Pralix and Damhadh-Duidas together face The Spider at Blencathra in Cumbria. Using her pre-Hermetic training, Pralix summons Lethe, a mighty spirit of forgetfulness. Lethe plucks the memory of the magical Pictish language from Damhan-Allaidh’s gruagachan, leaving them powerless and unable to cast the simplest spell. In the largest battle fought so far, Pralix’s soldiers fall on the Picts, while her magicians provide magical support. It’s the most decisive victory yet by Pralix. To Pralix’s great anger, Damhan-Allaidh escapes her clutches once again. In fury and frustration she blames Damhadh-Duidas for this failure, accusing him of colluding with the enemy. The maga and the sorcerer fight, first with words, then with spells. Damhadh-Duidas is driven away by Pralix’s assault and is not seen again.

*Pralix’s mighty working of magic nearly obliterated the entire magical tradition of *

*the gruagachan. The few Pictish magicians not present at the Battle of Blencathra were able to hold on to the fading threads of their magic, but they never truly recovered. *

The final clash between Pralix and Damhan-Allaidh occurs just a month after Blencathra, on the shores of the lake outside the camp. Damhan-Allaidh comes with everything he has left, and he is faced by nearly all of Pralix’s remaining magicians. As the sun drops, Pictish priests begin to summon the sluagh, while multi-armed giants hurl rocks and a ram-headed serpent dragon plows through the ranks of warriors. In response, Pralix’s wizards summon a massive ball of fire as bright as the sun, driving the ghosts back into the night. Her elementalists summon whirlwinds of fire and earth elementals who drag the giants under the ground. Beastmasters lead phalanxes of bears, wolves and stags against the dragon. Pralix herself is in the midst of battle, sword in one hand and spear in the other, as she fights her way through to Damhan-Allaidh’s redoubt. She had prepared for the moment that she would face him. The mage-smiths amongst her people had made her an unbreakable magic chain, forged from obscure ingredients and strange magics collected over several years. Upon facing Damhan-Allaidh at last, she shackles herself to her opponent with the chain, ensuring that the conflict can only end with one of their deaths. Her Hermetic Magic prevails against Damhan-Allaidh’s spellcraft and the wizard is finally slain.

*After the Battle of the False Sun (as it became known), Damhan-Allaidh’s body *

*was laid out for cremation but it disappeared overnight and the guards set over *

*the pyre were killed in a gruesome manner. No amount of divination revealed *

*what had happened to it. Convinced that Damhan-Allaidh’s magical allies were to *

*blame, Pralix had her magicians scouring every inch of Britain for everyone who *

*had supported Damhan-Allaidh. Each one was brought to her and their secrets *

*ripped from their minds before they were put to death. *

*During this action it is believed that every single member of the Saxon tradition of rune magic was exterminated, and the Scottish druids were diminished so badly that they guttered and faded a generation later. *

811: Ordo Miscellanea formed by Pralix filia Tytalus at Cad Gadu, and also sets up a new covenant taking the name. The new Ordo includes an array of British hedge magicians. Pralix may have intended her organization to become the "beloved rival" of the Order. "Cad Gadu" means "abandoned army" in Welsh, referring to Damhan-Allaidh's defeat.

*When Pralix declined to return to home in triumph, Mercere visited her in person at Cad Gadu, but was refused entrance. Pralix renounced both her House and the Order, and was now the leader of a new Order, the Ordo Miscellanea. It offered protection to all magicians who had been rejected or persecuted by the Order of Hermes because their powers were too paltry, or did not bear the legacy of mighty Rome. The outraged Order of Hermes called for the immediate*

*extirpation of both Pralix and her upstart order, but Hariste (Tytalus’s successor) and Trianoma both argued for a settlement.*

*While the Order of Hermes was paralyzed with indecision, the Ordo Miscellanea*

*recruited aggressively throughout northern and western Europe.*

811–816: Sporadic fighting as Damhan-Allaidh's followers are hunted down; Clan Mac Gruagach joins Ordo Miscellanea. The Northern Guard, Praeses Septentrionalis, formed out of several new covenants to maintain a vigilant watch for Damhan-Allaidh's return.

812: Bonisagus is forced to March his own filius, Jovius, a man accepted as apprentice in his twenties and sped through the complete Hermetic course in four years. Regrettably, Jovius lacked the maturity to be a magus even though he possessed the requisite lore, and within a few months of his gauntlet he was convicted of seriously breaching his Oath of Hermes.

*The experience changed Bonisagus, he left Durenmar and never took another apprentice.*

814: Death of Emperor Charlemagne, marking a decline in mortal scholarship. His empire divides into chaos in three mortal generations.

815 Thebes: Tremere covenant of Dostolon founded on the south bank of the Danube.

816 Iberia: Many of the magi of Duresca, mostly descendants of Vates and Tempestarii, but also some of the Diedne, declare their support for the Ordo Miscellanea.

817: After debate among the Founders and Primi, Trianoma travels to meet Pralix at Cad Gadu; Trianoma agrees to admit Ordo Miscellanea to the Order as House Ex Miscellanea.

*This move doubled the size of the Order at that time! Apparently the Clan Mac Gruagach breaks with Pralix, as they are also noted as joining House Ex Miscellanea much later in 987. *

*Under Pralix, the House was ruled by a Council of Four, representing different facets of the House’s interests. It was initially a martial House populated by battle-hardened wizards, and ruthlessly pursued the "join or die" imperative of the Order, making enemies of both Tremere and Flambeau, who felt that their own House identities were being threatened, by numbers if not power.*

**817 Fourth Grand Tribunal: The admission of House Ex Miscellanea confirmed. Maga Pralix is now Founder Pralix. The Tribunals of Hibernia, Loch Leglean and Stonehenge are created as part of the merger.**

**On Pralix advice, seven covenants are to be established in the former territory of Damhan-Allaidh. These are the Praesides Septentrionales, the "Bastions of the North," and their mission is to watch for the return of Damhan-Allaidh.**

**Diedne boasts that "there is not a single druid left in Ireland", almost certainly an exaggeration.**

**Mercere and Trianoma persuade the other magi of the Order to bring Redcaps into the Order.**

**Primus Quendalon of Merinita causes great ill-will between Houses Merinita and Tytalus by laughing loudly during their account of their Founder’s disappearance.  \
Quendalon announces his House’s new dedication to Faerie.**

**Many question Quendalon’s reports on the events that split his House and the expulsion of Myanar’s followers, but no one is able to contest his description of events.**

**Certamen is accepted as decisive in all disputes. **

*The second meeting of the Grand Tribunal. From this date to the Schism War there are Thirteen Houses of Hermes. *

*While there were several false alarms, particularly in the early days, there were*

*no further confirmed signs of The Spider, and one by one these special covenants abandoned their watch, faltered and died out. The very last in 1025.*

*This Grand Tribunal marks the last public appearance of Founder Guernicus, and it was against his strident objection that certamen was instituted as "decisive in all disputes". Guernicus retreated to his Magvillus fortress, claiming the ruin of the Order was at hand. It is rumored he later foreswore his Arts, shunned all contact with other magi (except his favored filius Fenicil) and undertook a pilgrimage to Rome.*

*The certamen ruling gave Tremere advantages in pivotal matters, as he had focused on becoming a master duelist. He certainly manufactured disputes, and then settled them with certamen. This hamstrung the Quaesitores, who became mere spectators to its consequence.*

*Quendalon later explained that he was not mocking Tytalus’s ambitious attempt to defeat "the Queen," but found it amusing that his followers would perceive his end as tragic. He gave them the impression that he knew more than he was saying, but would not speak of it further. Since then, House Merinita and House Tytalus have often been at odds, their poor relationship aggravated by the possibility that Quendalon might have known what happened to their Founder, *

*or even been somehow responsible for their loss.*

817 Loch Leglean: Holy Oak, a covenant of Diedne, founded.

817 Stonehenge: The now Nameless Covenant, of Diedne, founded on Anglesey. *The vile rites practiced here are best left buried. The name of the Nameless Covenant was struck from all records after the Schism War.*

C. 817: Magus Anegin, a Welsh bard-mage, lawgiver, and seer, who had fought with Pralix against Damhan-Allaidh, joins House Bonisagus instead of House Ex Miscellanea. His Line continues to be famous for its political and mediation abilities.

818: Mercere dies.

818 Stonehenge: Blackthorn covenant founded in south Cambria (Wales), sponsored by House Tremere.

*Obvious play at building a base for domination of the new Tribunal.*

819: Flavia filia Jerbiton reappears after a sixteen year absence. She refuses to say where she has been and begins to search for a covenant site. 

*Flavia’s absence has been the subject of speculation since.*

820: Founder Flambeau disappears. One morning in the spring of that year he left Val-Negra alone. Where he went after that is unknown. His first apprentice Apromor concludes the sahirs had ambushed and killed him, and assumes leadership of the House, becoming the first Primus.

*Flambeau’s Arts were never strong outside of his area of specialty. Due to a*

*weak Longevity Ritual, the Founder grew old and frail. Some magi, including his filia Elaine, believe he retired from magic and joined a monastery as he was a pious Christian throughout his life. *

*Flambeau’s famed talisman Fireheart, a flawless fire opal the size of a walnut, was never recovered and could still remain somewhere in or near the Pyrenees.*

*Apromor had reportedly grown apart from his master, and he encouraged non-Hermetic wizards to join the Order, accepting pagans and worked with Priamitus, Mercere’s first apprentice, to recreate the Cult of Mercury.*

820 Stonehenge: Flavia filia Jerbiton founds Rosalba in the Yorkshire Dales. 

821 Rome: The Second Verdi Competition.

821–823 Stonehenge: Concordia covenant founded to meld the magical traditions of House Ex Miscellanea. It fails and disbands in two years. 

822 Normandy: Bibracte covenant founded, located on Mount Beauvray in Burgundy. Vassal of Fudarus.

*Consisting of a small number of Diedne magi and a magus of House*

*Tytalus. The covenant resurrected a cult worshiping the Gaulish hero Vercingetorix; however, the magi gradually became obsessed with anti-Roman ideals, firstly outlawing Latin in favor of Brythonic, and ultimately refusing all dealings with magi from "Roman" Houses. This earned them many enemies, and, seizing on the weakness of Fudarus in the aftermath of the betrayal of House Tytalus, four Flambeau and Tremere magi from Montverte simultaneously declared Wizard War on the magi of Bibracte, accusing them of devil-worship. These actions resulted in the sacking and destruction of the covenant in 969.*

822 Iberia: Priamitus filius Mercere and Somnus ex Tremere convoke all the peninsular magi in Secunda, in a sort of unofficial regional Tribunal. 

Aedes Saturni is founded in Barcelona, and is chosen to guard the treasuries of the Hermetic Tribunal (collected by the local tax defined in 801). Diedne from Duresca found Mons Vindius, in coordination with the Norman Diedne of Bibracte and the Germanic Diedne of Sirmium.

822 Rhine Tribunal: The Laws of Hospitality and the Peregrinatores are established in the Rhine Tribunal’s Peripheral Code. Based on the traditional German guest-laws, a magus may request hospitality from any covenant established legally in the Rhine Tribunal. The covenant must formally publish the conditions of hospitality, which, if agreed to by the magus, guarantee him at least three, but no more than seven years at the covenant. The conditions of hospitality must be the same for all guests currently in residence, but may be otherwise changed by the covenant at any time that they have no guests. The guest is entitled only to that which is granted in the conditions of hospitality, and must agree to its strictures, which must include - but is not restricted to - a fee of three pawns of vis per year paid to the host.

*A later ruling allowed that the fee of vis paid by the guest could be exchanged for equivalent service, and a season’s work was deemed to be adequate recompense.*

*The laws of hospitality have certainly given the Rhine Tribunal a unique feature of traveling magi - typically around a sixth of its magi are peregrinatores.*

825: Death of Jerbiton.

826–854 Provencal: The hedge wizard Pendule, discovered by Founder Flambeau, refuses to join the Order. He is powerful enough to evade the fiery Founder and his followers who seek to destroy him. 

Eventually, Pendule tire of these games and adopts four Hermetic magi as his followers. No one knows exactly why he did this; perhaps he simply wanted to share his secrets with others before he died, or perhaps he desired to learn Hermetic magic theory from them. Two of these followers were from House 

Merinita, and having Initiated Faerie Magic from Quendalon they were able 

to master the illusion Mysteries Pendule taught - the others could not comprehend them. Afterwards, these Merinitae continued to teach the secrets to their followers as part of a House lineage.

*Rumors surround Pendule, whose sensual Imagonem magic is said to be diabolic or descended from Eastern traditions, but the effect of his teachings on his students are unknown.*

*Many comedic tales depicting Pendule as a trickster defeating the mighty and blustery Flambeau with wit and illusions have become part of Hermetic lore. Pendule’s followers are loathed by most magi of House Flambeau, and may also find themselves embroiled in Wizard’s Wars with them as a matter of principle.*

826 Hibernia: The covenant of Lámbaird founded by Petrifer and Raghallach of House Flambeau. The latter being  a controversial druid convert, first hunted by Founder Diedne and later suffering her declarations of Wizard War.

*It has been the home of a school of magical combat - The School of Raghallach - *

*since its inception, and has always been a covenant of House Flambeau.*

829: Regional Tribunal Meetings. 

830 Hibernia: The Diedne covenant of Vigil is raided by Viking runesmiths based in Dublin.

*The magi of Vigil recovered and for a while helped defend other covenants from these magical invaders. That was until the Diedne of Ireland, Vigil included, withdrew from Tribunal affairs in 851.*

830 Rome: Guernicus' fortress Magvillus is taken over and founded as a covenant shortly after his (rumored) death. It’s location is in a remote mountainous region in Southern Italy (part of the Kingdom of Sicily), well isolated from the mundane world. It is, or later becomes, domus magna of House Guernicus.

*This highly secretive covenant has nothing to do with local Hermetic politics, except through normal legal channels. Unless invited, only Quaesitors and Redcaps are allowed in.*

C. 831: Saracen conquest of Sicily.

831 Gathering of Twelve Years at Crintera: Birna speaks her final words to the magi of her House and disappears into the forest in bear-form.

C. 832 Iberia: One of Priamitus' sons, Don Sayban, organizes the Redcaps of the peninsula, conducting the first census of the peninsular covenants from Aedes Saturni. 

**832 Fifth Grand Tribunal: The Rules of Certamen are restricted to forbid the use of certamen to force a magus to violate the Code or give up his established rights, including the rights of praecones (leading a Tribunal), Primi (ruling a house), and Quaesitores (protecting the Order).**

**Apprentice Law ruling that a magus must provide his apprentice with three month’s training or risk losing that apprentice to another magus, if the apprentice agrees.**

**House Diedne representatives from Hibernia (Ireland) report troubling encounters against northern rune wizards who ravaged the whole Emerald Isle, and that Parma Magica only offers limited protection against them. Those who had heard of their pagan faith dubbed them members of "The Order of Odin". Uncertain about the rune wizards’ strength, the Order uncharacteristically decides to seek peace.**

**The Tribunal of Iberia is denied recognition as an independent Tribunal on the basis of the small number of peninsular magi present.**

**This is the last Grand Tribunal Bonisagus and Trianoma attended. They shared a quiet meal together and after supper Trianoma retired to her private suite and died in her sleep.**

**Fenicil becomes Primus of House Guernicus.**

**This is the second Tribunal to enter the thirty three year cycle. From henceforth, Grand Tribunals will meet every 33 years, regional Tribunals every 7 years from 836 AD. The seven year cycle is taken from Prokopios’ idea and the Theban practice.**

*It is rumored that Trianoma did not die peacefully, and that her spirits now haunt the Black Forest at Durenmar. The Tower of Trianoma at Durenmar is obviously crooked and has a ruined top. But it is said the two top floors were destroyed in some magical accident, and just never reconstructed.*


## The Age of Ambition and Intrigue (AD 833–910)

*With the last of the Founders departing from power, a new era begins - one of maneuvering, politicking, and hidden struggles. The unity of the Order remains intact, but the early vision of brotherhood among magi fades as covenants grow wealthy, secretive, and self-serving.*

*House Tremere rises in influence. No longer tempered by the Founders’ presence, Tremere’s successors begin consolidating power, using certamen, alliances, and legal rulings to control weaker magi.*

*The Great Houses compete for dominance. Tytalus magi weave intrigues, Jerbiton influence mundanes, and Flambeau and Bjornaer struggle over frontier covenants.*

*The first deep suspicions of House Diedne emerge. Their refusal to reveal their inner rites leads to whispers of dark pacts and hidden agendas.*

*By the late 9th century, Hermetic law is more defined than ever, but so are its loopholes, manipulations, and factional politics. The Grand Tribunal of 910 marks a turning point - major Tribunals have solidified, and many magi now view politics as a tool just as valuable as magic itself.*

*The Order is no longer just a society of scholars - it is a world of its own, filled with alliances, grudges, and the early signs of what will one day lead to war.*

834 Loch Leglean: Well of the Head, a covenant of Diedne, founded. 

836: Regional Tribunal Meetings. 

*Not much of note seems to remain. Bonisagus is last seen alive at the regional Thebes Tribunal gathering, recruiting magi for some kind of expedition.*

836 Iberia: Baldiccione ex Guernicus founds the covenant of Campus Stellae. Barzaj is officialized.

836 Rhine: Notatus becomes Primus of House Bonisagus and is selected Praeco of the Rhine Tribunal. 

*A shining champion of the superiority of Hermetic magic!*

Before 837: Final Twilight or Death of Founder Criamon.

*The manner of his passing is not known outside his House.*

837: Death of Founder Verditius.

*This leaves Founder Tremere as the last living Founder, excluding the later added Pralix.*

837 Hibernia: A delegation of primarily Irish magi from many different Houses, led by Máel-tuili of Merinita, seeks out the rune wizards (in the Order called "The Order of Odin") and attempts to parley with them. At first, the Viking leaders are abusive and demand to know their secrets, but after hours of negotiation the rune wizards eventually agree that they will leave in peace if the magi pay them a tribute of 3,000 pounds of silver and forty pawns of vis. This price is arranged, but soon after they have it in hand the Vikings renege on the deal and attack. The leaders of both parties fall in the subsequent battle, and many other magi and wizards are killed. The news of this betrayal stir equal parts of outrage and horror in the local magi and throughout the Order, but no immediate action is taken.

*The Vikings were worse in other areas and the Order’s attention shifted from Ireland. Many Hibernian magi hoped that the Ostmen threat would unite the Order in Ireland, especially patching over the quarreling between Houses Ex Miscellanea and Diedne, but they were disappointed.*

838 Rome: Rellantali covenant founded on Sardinia.

839 Rome: The Third Verdi Competition, the first after the death of Founder Verditius.

839 Stonehenge: Cad Gadu chronicles note the visit of a group of wandering magi. The wandering magi are noted in the records of Stonehenge from time to time hereafter.

*This may be the wandering covenant of Semitae, foundation date unknown.*

840 Normandy: Pagus covenant founded at a ruined Roman outpost near the town of Cherbourg, offering a commanding strategic vista over the English channel. Vassal of Confluensis.

*Destroyed in the Schism War 1008.*

840 Thebes: Covenant of Miphoria founded on the coastal outskirts of Smyrna, as the first all-Mercere covenant of the Tribunal.

Tremere magi seize Epidauros.

841 Rome: Calabria covenant founded.

843: The epic poem Travels of Fedoso completed by Verderis, third Primus of Criamon. Fedoso is a young man who travels through a strange landscape facing mystical obstacles, figurative enemies, and perplexing situations. He finds allies and companions, but ends his journey alone at the peak of a mountain.

*This classic work has been used by generations of masters to teach their apprentices Latin, may be a ciphered prediction of the course of the Order, and is the center of obscure debate. But of course you already know that…*

*Those versed in the text claim that it predicted the Corruption of House Tytalus and the Schism War. A few magi refuse to allow their students to read this book, as they believe it causes the calamities it predicts.*

*There are rumors about a lost final page of the book, which allegedly describes what Fedoso sees from the mountain.*

843: The Treaty of Verdun ends four years of civil war among the grandsons of Charlemagne, and his empire is divided among his three heirs.

843: Regional Tribunal Meetings.

*Some believe the Sundering was planned at the Theban Tribunal.*

843 Iberia: Tertia is constituted as another Tremere base of operations. 

Qatr al-Nada, Ranemiro's filia, establishes the covenant of Dar Maliha, with the purpose of reaching peace with the sahirs. 

844 Britannian Tribunal: The Chief Bard of Ireland, Cormacan the Learned, brought an embassy from High-King Congalach Cnogba to the Order. Some of the king’s subjects, hedge wizards all, had complained to him about harassment by the Order. Fedelmid Ua Heirméais of House Mercere was appointed to act on behalf of the Order. The result of these negotiations was the Treaty of Cnoc Maol Réidh. The hedge wizards of Connacht were incorporated into a federation that became known as Coill Trí, meaning the "three hazels".

*From Diedne’s pogroms it was already known that "Join or Die" didn’t work in Ireland; both draoithe and asarlaíthe would rather die. Since these hedge wizards had sought the protection of the high-king, another solution had to be found. It took until the first meeting of the Hibernian Tribunal in 898 to make it binding.*

845 Iberia: The Asturian king Ramiro I orders the execution of all sorcerers in his kingdom, probably being manipulated by Atropos ex Tremere. The edict affects mainly hedge wizards and magi Ex Miscellanea. Tancoystl Ex Miscellanea tries to stop the edict, even organizing a plan to assassinate Ramiro I, but is ambushed and executed.

845 Normandy: The Viking warlord Reginherus aided by rune wizards leads a substantial raiding party of 120 ships carrying thousands of warriors to lay siege to Paris. Charles the Bald, who is determined not to let the royal Abbey of Saint-Denis be destroyed, assembles an army. However, the Vikings are victorious, and they hang their prisoners of war on an island on the Seine in honor of Odin. They then plunder and occupy the city, withdrawing only after Charles pays an enormous ransom of 7,000 French livres in gold and silver. \
*Reginherus is also known as Ragnar Lodbrok. This was a dangerous precedent set by Charles the Bald of paying a Viking leader off for peace, as it only encouraged more raids. Vikings also sack the cities of Hamburg and Melun later that year. *

846: Saracen raiders plunder the outskirts of Rome, sacking the basilicas of Old St Peter's and St Paul's-Outside-the-Walls, but are prevented from entering the city itself by the Aurelian Walls.

847: A major council is held in south Francia in response to the Saracen raid, attended by many bishops and by Emperor Lothar I and King Louis II of Italy. A capitulary is issued and, with contingents from Francia, Burgundy and Provence and his own Italian forces, Louis launches a successful campaign against the Saracens occupying Benevento. \
*The Saracen survivors were made prisoners, enslaved and sent to work in chain gangs building the Leonine Wall to encompass the Vatican Hill. Rome is never again threatened by an Arab army.*

**848: The Sundering of Tremere. Founder Tremere, last living Founder, has almost succeeded in dominating the Order (and declaring himself Overlord) through political intrigue and application of certamen over the previous decade, but is stopped in this year by a band of magi who strike his chief lieutenants by afflicting their minds. This strike was performed all at once by magi who penetrated the Founder’s covenant of Dorostolon in the Theban Tribunal and broke key elements of the lieutenants’ minds.**

**While Tremere did negotiate peace terms with his Sundering enemies with the intervention of the Primus of House Guernicus, his memories of the event were removed with his consent as part of the agreement, and therefore the identities of the Sunderers remain unknown.**

*This is a pivotal event in Hermetic history; the first serious inter-House conflict, the first known resolution outside the Code, and the first major negotiated settlement. Members of House Jerbiton claim or are suspected of taking part in the Sundering, as is Albanus, the second Primus of House Tremere.*

C. 848 Iberia: Secunda is dissolved; Somnus loses his mind during the Sundering and the rest of the Tremere leave the covenant. Tertia is not dissolved, but Atropos is also annulled.

848 Thebes: Tremere covenant of Dostolon destroyed in the Sundering, allegedly ripped apart by a Typhoon. Tremere forced to relinquish Epidauros.

After 848 Stonehenge: Blackthorn covenant, allied with House Tremere, suffers a drop in political power following the Sundering. Rosalba covenant seeks to further this weakening. This decline leads to the harsh policies of Blackthorn in the coming centuries.

849 Loch Leglean: Covenant of Peanhafel, inhabited by Pictish magi of House Ex Miscellanea destroyed by Diedne magi in support of a mortal king. No known reprisal-woe that Diedne was not chastised before it descended further.

850-948 Hercynius, the penultimate apprentice of Bonisagus, establishes a lineage of Bonisagus magi, and attracts others to his teachings.

850-932: Life of Isaac Ben Solomon Israeli, a Tunisian physician in the employ of al-Mahdi, the founder of the Fatimid dynasty. Influenced by the works of Plato, Isaac was a monist, believing that all of creation was simply emanations from a single unknowable truth. These beliefs contributed to his study of the fledgling Kabbalah, and he became an accomplished kabbalist.

*It’s unfortunate we never got to see this man join the Order, and we have yet to incorporate the secrets of Kabbalah.*

850: Regional Tribunal Meetings.

851 Britannian Tribunal: After the predations of the Viking rune wizards of the Order of Odin, as well as great tensions between native wizards and Hermetic magi (skirmishes and disputes over resources were a constant problem), the Order agrees to acknowledge the Coill Trí, a confederation of hedge wizards in Connacht who had earned the title of druid, and to conclude a peace agreement with them - the Treaty of Cnoc Maol Réidh. The Diedne Primus Obregon declares that the magi of his House would "no longer protect our undeserving sodales from the enemy," but instead would "respect the Code from within the safety of our secret mounds and hidden dales". 

*Many of the magi in the northern Tribunals felt that it was the interference of Máel-tuili of Merinita that had endangered them all and brought ruin upon their lands. Instead of joining in war against the rune wizards, most of these magi went into hiding, especially the magi of House Diedne.*

*Nearly all interaction between covenants and magi in the region broke down for about 20 years, and remained sporadic for much of the following century, but forever after the Irish Diedne remained apart from their sodales and never participated in the local Tribunals again.*

851 Normandy: Baiocassium covenant founded in the forest near Bayeux. A vassal of Branugurix.

*The magi of Baiocassium, predominantly of House Diedne, were the descendants of Gallic druids and had good relationships with the local faeries. Destroyed in the Schism war 1011.*

851 Rome: The Tribunal is subjected to a violent earthquake.

*It is not known if this was a natural earthquake.*

852 Hibernia: With House Diedne having abandoned Ireland to its fate, a band of Merinita magi sacks Vigil covenant and takes the boar tusk cathac to the far north of Ireland. The Merinita raise a fortress, the new covenant of Vigil, and a great unseen tower, from the top of which all Ireland can be seen. They use its magic to find and intercept the rune wizards and their Viking warriors.

*Vigil struck quickly when the Schism War broke out, launching a devastating attack on their age-old enemies. They took Diedne heads as trophies, raided their laboratories, ransacked their libraries, and slaughtered their familiars.*

852 Stonehenge: Castrum Antiquum founded in the ruins of a Roman fort on Hadrian’s Wall.

855 Normandy: Sinapis covenant founded, located in Anjou near the center of France.

*Abandoned in 1063, as its members left for Provence and Iberia.*

856 Thebes: An earthquake at Corinth kills tens of thousands.

*Natural or not?*

857: Regional Tribunal Meetings.

857 Iberia: The covenant of Inverneira is founded.

857 Rhine Tribunal: The spirits of three of the Tribunal’s founding magi, believed lost to Twilight, suddenly made themselves known and demanded the right to vote on an important and contentious issue. The gathering of magi was shocked, but the Praeco ruled that the spirits may cast their votes, out of the great respect held for these elder magi.  The three extra votes were enough to tip the balance and the motion was wisely defeated, saving the Tribunal from strife. Before departing, each of the three designated one of their filii to cast their vote by proxy at future Tribunal gatherings. 

857 Theban Tribunal: Founding of covenant of Erebos, following a diplomatic initiative by Pralix. It’s dedicated to the preservation of traditions of magic that are native to the Theban Tribunal. 

857 Rome: The Fourth Verdi Competition.

858 Provencal: Founded around a mithraeum at the site of an old Roman 

camp at the mouth of the Adour in Gascony, the Flambeau covenant of Lapurdum (822–858) is abandoned under repeated pressure from Viking raids and internal dissension.

C. 858 Rhine: Sometime in the late 850’s two Bjornaer magi - Wolfgang and Adler were led to a place of supernatural power by a Great Beast, a lion. In the  wild and sparsely inhabited forested hills of Sauerland, near Dortmund and Cologne they establish the covenant of Collem Leonis. 

*The covenant’s chief diplomats, Pamprepius of Flambeau and Tacitus of Tremere, reached a mutually beneficial agreement with the local lord, whereby the forested hills were designated as hunting grounds and thereby restricting interaction with the mundane.*

*Always a secretive covenant and there are many rumors surrounding it, often including magical beasts and portals to mysterious regiones. It has been a winter covenant for decades and not involved in Tribunal affairs.*

C. 860 Loch Leglean: Activities of giants in Scotland monitored; found to be no danger to the Order.

C. 861–862: Death of Tremere, last of the Founders, as all the Founders now dead, inactive or missing. Rebuffed by the Sundering, he’s seen as a somewhat sinister figure in the modern Order. He is the only Founder that is known to have been prosecuted for a violation of his Hermetic Oath. The second meeting of the First Tribunal in 773 found him innocent.

*Finally he is gone!*

862 Transylvania: Tremere conference (presumably at Coeris). This once in a decade meeting brings together the House for coordination of plans and operations. Probably the first ten-year meeting, the year after their Founder’s death. Primus Albanus, his successor, effectively uses anger directed at Tremere’s legacy to solidify the House under his leadership. 

*Oh, if only one could have been there, for the pure entertainment of watching the endless, headless debates and power-grabbing machinations.*

863: Prima Pralix of House Ex Miscellanea disappears while returning from a visit to her former domus magna Fudarus.

*Many suspect members of Houses Flambeau and/or Tremere of foul play. Without her firm guidance, House Ex Miscellanea gradually lost its former coherence as each tradition of magi pursued their own agenda without any thought towards the House as a whole.*

863 Iberia: Alma Mater is dissolved after Tello's execution (along with the Martyrs of Córdoba).

864: Bulgaria converted to Christianity as Boris I of Bulgaria is baptized by ambassadors of Byzantine Church.

864: Regional Tribunal Meetings.

864 Rhine Tribunal: Following the precedent from previous Tribunal meeting, a handful of elder magi arrive at the Tribunal bearing the voting sigils of their ancient parentes, in or at the verge of Final Twilight, claiming that they were merely "ill-disposed to attend." A small number of other elder magi formally designate a follower to bear their sigil by proxy in the event of their being "unable to attend future meetings." 

The covenant of Collem Leonis is ratified as the eighth covenant of the newly named Rhine Tribunal without facing significant political resistance.

The Iberian Quaesitors Baldiccione and Qatr al-Nada ex Guernicus come from Iberia to denounce the use of an improvised Peripheral Code, especially among the northern covenants, against the decision of the Great Tribunal of 832. They ask the Rhine for support to regularize the situation by the constitution of an official Iberian Tribunal, and the establishment of another truce with the sahirs. Their proposals are accepted, and the Rhine magi agree to take the request for an Iberian Tribunal to the Grand Tribunal, but abrogate the unofficial Code and confiscate the vis guarded by Aedes Saturni until the situation is regulated.

865 Stonehenge: A great army of Vikings, aided by rune wizards of Order of Odin, lands in East Anglia and proceeds through kingdom after kingdom, subduing or killing everyone who opposes them.

**865 Sixth Grand Tribunal: The boundaries of the Tribunals are formally set according to the break-up of the Frankish Empire, they are also named as the Rhine, Frankish (Normandy), Lotharingian (Provençal), Britannian, Roman, Alpine (Greater Alps), Theban, Oriental (Eastern, or Levant) Tribunals.**

*This is the first big topic, carefully vetted and prepared by the Primi beforehand. Most Britannian magi are unhappy for being thrown together into one regional Tribunal, but are not taken overly seriously on large scale organizational issues.*

*Offended by the confiscation of the Iberian Treasure, very few peninsular magi went to the Great Tribunal, and their request to constitute the Tribunal of Iberia is denied for lack of quorum.*

**Primus Fenicil of Guernicus presents on the topic of preventing misuse of certamen. Certamen cannot be used to defy a Tribunal judgment, make a magus break the Code of Hermes or ignore a Code violation. This is mainly motivated by Tremere’s take-over attempt.**

**Hibernian magi raise the problem that House Diedne does not recognize the Treaty of Cnoc Maol Réidh with the local hedge wizards, but are told their treaty is illegal as they are not part of the Order. Furthermore, Ireland is included with England and Scotland in the Britannian Tribunal, and Hibernian magi are sternly told they have to follow the Britannian Peripheral Code. Already displeased, the Irish contingent argue how important the Treaty of Cnoc Maol Réidh really is: When given the choice between joining or dying, most Irish wizards will gladly fight and die, a course of action that will destroy the native magical traditions. The treaty allows the traditions to continue, perhaps to be explored and incorporated into Hermetic magic. Invading magi threatens that accord. The Grand Tribunal still refuses to recognize the treaty.**

**Magus Hercilion reprimanded, but not punished, for serving as a court wizard. The Grand Tribunal found that such a position endangers the Order as other mundane rulers will soon want their own court wizards, and they may become angry at those who refuse such roles. It is also a disgrace and beneath the dignity of a true Hermetic magus to serve a mundane. Henceforth, no magus shall ever serve a mundane as a servant or hireling.**

*This ruling was to have serious repercussions on those who live with a mundane lord. Magi are bound by this ruling not to work as "court wizards," yet they have a natural desire to support the titular owner of their home, magically or mundanely. If they live in a covenant that is situated in a manor or castle, they will wish to use magic to improve living conditions and further their own interests against potential mundane threats. To what extent does this run the risk of breaching the Code? *

*Iola of Guernicus determines the distinction in 1046 that the vital aspect is that even if one lives within the actual home of a mundane noble, no vassal relationship exists. Court wizards are generally defined as "employees," but employee here is not a function of economic support, but rather a relationship of subordination enforced by vows (usually of vassalage).*

**Status of the dead magi in the Rhine: Discussion of three Rhine Tribunal magi supposed to be in Final Twilight before their spirits appeared 857 at Tribunal, demanding and receiving the right to vote. Notatus tries to ride a wave of consent and goodwill from previous votes, and argues to legalize the recent Rhine practice of voting with the sigils of Hermetic ancestors most probably in final Twilight. He might have been overconfident about this, though - and barely escapes with a "no decision" by the Tribunal.**

**Conquests of territory by the Order of Odin, endangering covenants:**

**The Order of Odin has betrayed and killed negotiators of the Order of Hermes in Ireland, entrapped and killed Hoplites of the Order in Gascony, and is now sending a great heathen army into England to subdue it. An urgent response of the Order is requested by the English covenants, in particular Blackthorn. Notatus asks for the opinions of the covenants concerned by this. And Flavia of Jerbiton, representing her covenant Rosalba near York, explicitly requests to be left alone in handling the Vikings. When it then turns out that Blackthorn is an all-Tremere covenant at the border to Wales, nearly two-hundred miles away from the army’s landing site, the motion is defeated.**

**Pagans, Muslims and the Order: Hariste moves, that, since the heathens now already threaten the new Britannian, West Franks, Rhine, Roman, Theban and Eastern Tribunals, only Christians be henceforth allowed into the Order. She also moves that every apprentice, before taking the Oath, shall from now on recite the Nicene creed. The debate gets heated, but at the end the proposal gets downvoted.**

*To the glee of the Tytali. Notatus - seeing Blackthorn’s shameful defeat and guessing that it will have consequences - has sent in the clowns. Hariste’s motion alludes to the Flambeaus’ recruiting practices and tries to bait them.*

866 Stonehenge: Rector Maris covenant founded on the Isle of Man. It is destroyed within six months, apparently by the Order of Odin. This is the first known attempt to establish a covenant on Man.

*It is tempting to assign this destruction to magi of Diedne, as one of their main covenants was on the nearby island of Anglesey.*

C. 867: The first meeting of the Archmagi of the Order, one hundred years after the Founding. They discuss many subjects, supposedly charting out the future of the Order. The only known result is an agreement to create a tomb for their deceased members. The covenant of Magvillus (Rome) is charged with protecting the tomb of the Archmagi.

*The reported charting of the Order’s future is unlikely to include such disasters as the Corruption, the Schism War, or the vampirization of Tremere. The influence of the council of Archmagi is probably much exaggerated.*

867 Thebes: Peasant revolt in Anatolia leads to many cities being sacked, and widespread Paulician heresy among the rebels.

870–930 Iceland: The era of the landnam (land-taking). Main colonization sees 10-20 thousand Norsemen claim the available agricultural land.

*The Irish priests decide not to return to the island.*

870 Iberia: The sahirs agree to the conditions of the truce arranged by Baldiccione ex Guernicus. The Iberian covenants prosper under the new conditions of peace.

870 Rome: Potestas covenant founded in northern Italy.

871 Iberia: Diedne from Duresca found the covenants of Nebula and Ipagrum. Tremébriga is founded as an example of collaboration between the Tremere and the Order. Ziyad ex Jerbiton establishes Fafhs al-Luyy, and Hermogenes formalizes Ajabeba.

871 Normandy Tribunal: "The inaugural meeting of the Tribunal of the West Franks". Present are the covenants of Confluensis, Pagus, Baiocassium, Bibracte, and Sinapis. 

*The covenants of Fudarus and Branugurix both attend the Britannian Tribunal meeting instead.*

871 Rhine Tribunal: The sigils of many former magi have returned to (or stayed in) usage, descending the Tribunal conference into chaos and controversy. Some of these sigils are permitted, others not, depending largely on favor and patronage.

871 Stonehenge: Alfred the Great becomes king of Wessex and sets out to drive the Danes back.

*By 886 he had regained control of London and the fealty of all the English in the south and west of the country, saving English culture from disappearing beneath the Viking tide. Something he could never have accomplished without Hermetic assistance.*

872 Transylvania: Tremere conference at Coeris.

873 Stonehenge: Wizard’s War between Blackthorn maga Diana of Tremere and Rosalba magus William of Jerbiton. Diana is the victor after the death of William. A period of skirmishing follows despite the efforts of Flavia of Rosalba.

875: Fenicil, the first Primus of House Guernicus declares the conclusions of his long study of ancient magical history; that the Order is a manifestation of a magical tradition that goes back to the beginning of history.

*Why Fenicil declares the Order a manifestation, rather than an inheritor, is unclear. Fenicil assembled an extensive collection of pre-Hermetic magic, including what are believed to be Egyptian rituals. This collection includes powerful, secret rituals now known as Fenicil's Rituals, and is located at what has come to be called Fenicil's Library, at Magvillus, domus magna of Guernicus.*

*According to legend, Fenicil discovered a strange letter that resembled the Malleus Epistula while he was searching for ancient knowledge he could use to unite his House. It is said that he decided the letter was diabolical, and instead of incorporating it into the secret rituals, he burned it. Later, he described it to some of his followers as a dangerous idea that might be used against magi. He is remembered as saying that "when fools have a hammer, everything seems a nail to them; this evil letter is a witch-hammer, and so it will find witches everywhere." In reference to this story, some Quaesitors refer to overzealous inquisitors as "witch-hammers", and thus it is the closest thing to a name this tradition possesses.*

*Some Quaesitors fear that Fenicil did not in fact burn the letter.*

875 Normandy: Rotomagus covenant founded near Rouen by the first generation of apprentices from Sinapis.

*It was destroyed less than a quarter of a century later in 892 when the Vikings sacked Rouen. The sole surviving member left House Flambeau in shame, and joined House Ex Miscellanea.*

875 Rome: The Fifth Verdi Competition.

875 Stonehenge: Covenant of Sursum founded on the north coast of Cornwall.

C. 875 Rome: Roman magi record in this period that magic has seemed to dwindle.

*This dwindling sets off a century of infighting among the covenants and magi of the Tribunal.*

C. 875: Juden of Verditius slays many clergy on the island of Sardinia, thinking to limit the influence of the Dominion and revive vis sources. His victims are held up as martyrs and he is Marched. Executed by a Flambeau Hoplite, his death by fire is credited to God by the mortal witnesses.

*Juden is barely the worst of the lot. Many other magi lash out against mundanes and each other in this period.*

877: The Order hosts the First Secular Feast (every 120 years).

878 Iberia: Gamila ex Jerbiton establishes the covenant of Qasr Dar al-Muchaddad with the aim of introducing high Islamic culture to the Order.

878 Rhine Tribunal: "As the First Tribunal has famously witnessed, the spirits of the Founders and our most venerable and wisest magi are still with us. The study of magic may truly grant us a kind of immortality, although to what higher place we transcend we cannot know. It is therefore acknowledged that the voting sigil of each magus remains valid in perpetuity, until such time as evidence of his death is presented. Nevertheless, we cannot permit the First Tribunal to become dominated by the voices of those who are no longer active in its leadership. To this end, a magus whom no other has witnessed for a period of two Tribunals or more shall be  deemed to be retired. The sigil of a retired magus may only be held by proxy by a magister or Archmagus; a magister may hold only one such sigil, an Archmagus at most two. We entrust the duty of the witnessing of magi and the  reporting of the deaths of magi to our loyal servants, the Redcaps."

*The Tribunal was forced to make a firm and final ruling on the issue. It could not go against its own precedent and outlaw these proxy sigils, yet at the same time there was a clear need (and much urging from the Quaesitores) to reign in their number and the associated abuses. Strictly regulating their number according*

*to the rank of the wielder, seems to have solved the issue.*

*In support of this ruling, the Rhine Tribunal has developed an informal tradition of never fully recognizing the passing of magi into Twilight. There are various euphemisms for Final Twilight and retired magi (most of whom are lost to it) are  spoken of in the present tense, especially when their votes are cast. *

881: Charles "the Fat" reunites Charlemagne’s empire, becoming emperor. By now his realm is beset by attacks from Norsemen.

882 Transylvania: Tremere conference at Coeris.

884 Rome Tribunal: Gravis of House Flambeau is charged with slaying Talus of House Merinita. Gravis claims forfeit immunity as Talus had cast a spell on him. Renowned for his practical jokes, Talus had cast a spell that made Gravis’s voice

sound like a little girl’s, to the great mirth of all others present. Gravis responded with a Ball of Abyssal Flame, which slew Talus. As Talus’s spell was clearly not a threat to Gravis’s life or magic the Tribunal finds him guilty, but due to the clear provocation refrained from calling a Wizard’s March. Gravis is punished by the death of his familiar.

885 Normandy: Paris is again besieged by Viking raiders.

885 Normandy Tribunal (3rd): Maga Adela of Jerbiton, a frequent attendant at court, is accused of acting as "court wizard" to King Louis III between 879 and 881. She maintains that the accusation is brought by a magus with whom she had a long-standing feud, and there is no evidence to suggest any risk to the Order resulting from her friendship with the king, who died in 882. The maga is acquitted.

885 Rhine Tribunal: "We cannot prosper and overcome our enemies if our covenants war amongst themselves. So that we may enjoy peace forthwith, the First Tribunal will no longer recognize any covenant that has not gained the approval of its peers. Any prospective covenant is therefore required to obtain a sponsor from each and every existing covenant, who shall publicly proclaim their petition worthy. Should even one such sponsor not be forthcoming, the Tribunal will refuse recognition."

The magi of Arae Flaviae covenant in Swabia objected to the nearby Schwarzburg covenant, and their resulting warfare caused the Tribunal to abolish both covenants and rule that any covenant could veto the establishment of a new covenant. It is established that prospective covenants are required to obtain the explicit permission of all the Tribunal’s other covenants, in order to obtain recognition. If a covenant is not successful in getting approval from the Tribunal, they may try again in seven years time. Until they receive approval, they have no legal recourse against the depredations of other covenants against their resources - they do not exist, in the eyes of the Tribunal. The magi of an unapproved covenant thus have no communal rights, although they retain their rights as individuals.

*Needless to say, a prospective covenant may ﬁnd itself bound into all sorts of obligations, duties, and pacts in return for support, but some covenants make low demands in return for future favors. *

887: Charles "the Fat" is deposed and the Frankish empire is split into half a dozen kingdoms, most of which are ruled by Norse kings.

887: Belin, "the ideal Redcap" and last apprentice of Mercere the Founder dies. Known for her reliability and devotion to duty, her legend grows with each passing century. 

*She founded a bloodline and Societas of respected messengers. These redcaps usually refer to themselves as Followers of Belin.*

888 Normandy: Odo, Count of Paris, is chosen as king of the Franks on account of his brave and skillful resistance of the Norse invaders.

888 Rome: Auctus covenant destroyed by magi of Verdi.

889 Loch Leglean: Several covenants of Praeses Septentrionalis destroyed by followers of Damhan-Allaidh, starting with Heart's Delight; Flambeau and Tytalus magi sent to fight Damhan-Allaidh. Little else is known about the Praeses Septentrionalis covenants.

889 Provencal: Two rogue Tytalan magi, who specialize in war magic and leading a band of raiding Andalusian Moors, founds the covenant of Fraxinetum (889–975) in the bleak basalt Massifs de Maures near the south coast.

*They cause the most bitter and defining struggle in the east of the Tribunal by leading attacks across the Arelat region, and they provoke a series of disputes regarding the Peripheral Code and interference with mundanes. *

890 Loch Leglean: Damhan-Allaidh defeated at Caithness, but he escapes. Rumor persistently places the Shetland Islands as Damhan-Allaidh’s base.

890 Stonehenge: Flavia filia Jerbiton, founder of Rosalba, passes into Final Twilight, one day after finishing her final tractatus. Her covenant of Rosalba enters a new period of war with Blackthorn.

*Flavia carefully prepared for her Final Twilight, leading to speculation that she had discovered a great secret about "the last veil".*

890 Thebes Tribunal: Morwena of Ex Miscellanea brings charges against Darius of Guernicus, for abuse of Quaesitorial privilege. Morwena claims Darius had used his position as Quaesitor to conduct a vendetta against her for over thirty years. Morwena cites twenty-one separate investigations that Darius had conducted into her activities since she arrived in the Tribunal. Only four had come before Tribunal and only one had resulted in a conviction, and this was for a low crime. Seven investigations that initially focused on her eventually led to other magi. Darius claims he had good cause for his suspicions, as Morwena’s magical heritage is renowned for its dark practices. The Tribunal acquits Darius. *But he’s called to Magvillus less than a year later and then moved to the Rhine Tribunal.*

891: Iamitu’krielle of Criamon apparently passes into Final Twilight. Nevertheless she appears to magi thereafter to make strange prophecies.

*How convenient for them…*

891 Rome Tribunal: Dominicus of House Jerbiton is charged with the slaying of Gravis of House Flambeau. Dominicus claims forfeit immunity applies. Gravis had thrown a Ball of Abyssal Flame at him after a council discussion had become ‘heated’. Witnesses testify that Gravis had made the first attack, with only verbal provocation. Although his Parma was penetrated, Dominicus was barely injured and replied with Clenching Hand of the Crushed Heart, instantly slaying Gravis. A member of Dominicus’s covenant claims that Dominicus, an amicus of Talus, had taken a powerful potion that morning. This potion made his flesh impervious to fire; he then orchestrated the confrontation. Dominicus does not dispute this, but maintains that Gravis had attempted to kill him, forfeiting his immunity. The Presiding Quaesitor pronounce that Dominicus’s intention did not excuse Gravis’s action and he is acquitted.

*However, Dominicus was later convicted of breaching his covenant charter and was exiled from the Tribunal.*

892 Normandy: Covenant of Rotomagus is destroyed as Vikings sack Rouen.

*The sole surviving member left House Flambeau in shame, and joined House Ex Miscellanea.*

892 Rhine Tribunal: The First Tribunal sponsors a proposal for a new covenant, Rudiaria, affiliated to House Flambeau, which is duly founded. The Tribunal asserts Rudiaria’s ownership of all magical sites along the river Rhine between the towns of Mainz and Cologne.

892 Transylvania: Tremere conference at Coeris.

893 Rome: The Sixth Verdi Competition.

893–897 Stonehenge: Covenant Occulta founded by new magi, but withers in three years after failing to secure resources.

896 Stonehenge: London covenant founded.

897: Pope Formosus remains is exhumed and put on trial in the judgement known as Cadaver Synod. The corpse disinterred, clad in papal vestments, and seated on a throne to face all the charges from John VIII. His brief reign as pope had been troubled, and the verdict is that the deceased had been unworthy of the pontificate. All his measures and acts are annulled and the orders conferred by him are declared invalid. The papal vestments torn from his body, the three fingers from his right hand that he had used in consecrations are cut off and the corpse unceremoniously thrown into the Tiber (later retrieved by a monk).

*What happened to divine auras caused by this pope's blessings?*

**898 Seventh Grand Tribunal: Quaesitors required to maintain accreditation in order to officiate at Tribunal, specifically the magus must be known to at least one other magus of the Tribunal, and must present a document of good standing signed by Primus Guernicus and dated not more than seven years past.**

**Following the Tytalus example of Tribunal secession, the Irish magi declare that they leave the Britannian Tribunal, which splits into the new Tribunals of Stonehenge, Hibernia and Loch Leglean.**

**The Iberian Tribunal is officially constituted with Baldiccione ex Guernicus as its first Praeco.**


## The Tide of Mars (AD 898–1018)

*By the end of the 9th century, the last of the Founders had died or vanished into Twilight. The great names who once held sway were gone, leaving the Order in the hands of newly rising Primi and the recently recognized Archmagi who inherited much of the Founders’ social prestige. Outwardly, this seemed a time  of relative calm: Hermetic law continued to be refined, new covenants appeared, and the unity of the Order of Hermes had endured. Yet beneath that surface, old resentments and new intrigues were growing into something far more dangerous.*

*Rumors of Damhan-Allaidh’s return rippled through Loch Leglean, spurring fears that the infamous "Spider" had not truly perished in 810. Meanwhile, Wizard’s Wars - once individualized affairs - began escalating into broader covenant conflicts. Tensions spiked when disturbing documents known as the Duresca Scrolls were discovered, raising doubts about the intentions of House Guernicus and shaking trust in the Quaesitores at precisely the moment the Order needed them most.*

*Amid the backdrop of rivalries and simmering tensions across the Order, House Tytalus plunged into its most perilous gambit. Always devoted to testing boundaries - and certain they could toy with diabolic forces as adroitly as they outwitted rival magi - a powerful faction drifted into outright diabolism, a practice they concealed for years using the same cunning that once saw Tytalus survive the Sundering. Their hubris caught up with them during the Special Tribunal of 961, when Quaesitors led a purge that nearly eradicated the entire House. Although Tytalus weathered this maelstrom, the scandal not only crippled the House for decades but also *sent shockwaves through the entire Order and *rekindled old suspicions fed by earlier crises, such as the Spider War and the first tremors of the Schism. *

*In the wake of this debacle, many within the Order regarded Tytalus’s "games" less as bold innovation and more as ruinous overreach - reminders that even the craftiest manipulator can be undone by the foes they presume to master.*

*Mistrust of House Diedne intensifies. Their once-tolerated secrecy and druidic rites invite suspicion of hidden sins, and paranoid whispers multiply among the Latin Houses, in particular Tremere, Flambeau, and Jerbiton. Tensions mount, propelled by rumors of twisted sacrifices and Infernal bargains. Many covenants quietly close ranks, forging martial pacts or stockpiling vis, bracing for the war they sense is coming.*

*Even so, this era saw rapid growth of the Order. New covenants sprang up across Mythic Europe, House Ex Miscellanea integrated more hedge traditions, and promising young magi filled the gaps left by aging elders. But these gains came with growing pains. Heightened rivalries, Wizard’s Wars fought over unclaimed vis, and whispered accusations against Diedne rattled the fragile peace. Increasingly, disputes that might once have been settled by politics or Certamen now escalated into open conflict.*

*By the turn of the millennium, the Order’s outward prosperity masked a landscape of looming dread. Skirmishes erupt in border covenants, and the Quaesitors struggle to keep the peace. Some Houses clamor for an official condemnation of Diedne; others call for calm and proof. Yet, cooler heads are quickly drowned out as fear becomes the coin of Hermetic politics. Wizards’ War - once carefully declared in single grudges - now flares between rival covenants and entire factions.*

* The stage was set for the greatest crisis in Hermetic history: a fiery maelstrom of suspicion and violence - soon known as the Schism War - that would erupt in 1003 and forever reshape the Order of Hermes.*

*The accusations levied against House Diedne serve as the spark, but the true blaze of the Schism War is fueled by older feuds and unresolved betrayals. Over nine harrowing years, the Order tears at itself from within. The devastation culminates in the destruction of House Diedne. By 1018, the Order stands bloodied and hollow: once again united on parchment, but haunted by the echoes of an age when sodalis turned upon sodalis.*

898 Hibernia Tribunal (1st): The Treaty of Cnoc Maol Réidh with the Coill Trí hedge wizards in Connacht is finally made binding. The Tribunal encourages wizards who are in conflict with other magi over resources to relocate to Connacht, and the Order pledges support to facilitate the move. 

*Not all relocations were entirely voluntary. The end result was to concentrate the Irish hedge traditions into one province, leaving the rest of Ireland to the Hermetic magi, relatively free from competition with locals.*

*The Coill Trí is by no means a rival to the Order of Hermes. It is merely a legal entity with which the Order could make a single treaty, rather than hundreds of separate treaties with each individual tradition or draoi in Connacht.*

*Today, the Coill Trí boasts a membership at least equal to that of the Hibernian Order of Hermes, but possibly much larger. There are some who try to make the Coill Trí more than it is, to use it as a force to mobilize the native magicians of Ireland for some deeper purpose. These attempts usually fail because of The Gift, which forms a wedge of suspicion and distrust that drives any temporary alliance apart. The Order is more than happy with this arrangement.*

The Mercere Houses Leth Moga and Leth Cuinn are formed, as byproducts of Hibernia’s new independent Tribunal status.

*Since its beginning Leth Moga has been interested in preserving the Tribunal’s history. While the northern redcaps practice martial feats, the southern redcaps are the eye-witnesses of the Tribunal, keeping detailed journals of their accounts. Like Leth Cuinn, Leth Moga is not a covenant. It does not keep a cathach nor need a wealth requirement. It exists because it serves the Tribunal, and its existence is safeguarded by numerous Hibernia Peripheral Code rulings.*

899 Iberia: The Mercer House of Occelum Durii is established. Salionca establishes the chapter house of Izaro.

The mysterious covenant of Caium is founded.

899 Normandy Tribunal (5th): The Tribunal votes to support the decision of the 7th meeting of the Grand Tribunal, subsumes some of the former territories of the Provençal Tribunal and extends eastward to share a border with the Rhine Tribunal.

899 Stonehenge: Alfred the Great dies. His son Edward takes on the task of conquering the Danes and bringing the land back under English control, helped by his sister Aethelflaed. 

899 Stonehenge: Blackthorn covenant declares Wizard’s War on Rosalba covenant. Rosalba is destroyed and all the magi killed. One Blackthorn magus is killed by magical defenses. The magically protected site is abandoned and eventually lost.

899 Rhine: Emperor Arnulf dies suddenly. Two hedge wizards are accused of inflicting stroke upon him and executed.

900 Loch Leglean: Diedne spells eradicate the Pictish language, possibly to support the conquest of Kenneth MacAlpine, a mortal king.

*Further evidence of Diedne corruption.*

902 Transylvania: Tremere conference at Coeris.

906 Iberia: Mare Iacum founded.

906 Provencal: The original Mercer House and Mercurian covenant controlling the old Roman pass from the Piedmont into Gaul, Portus Cottiae (821-906), is abandoned after repeated attacks by Berber mercenaries in the late ninth century.

907 Iberia: Ruiz Sanchez ex Flambeau leads a group of magi dissatisfied with the current Tribunal's policy against Baldiccione and Qatr al-Nada. They ambush Qatr al-Nada during a diplomatic meeting with the Sahirs and kill everyone, breaking the truce between the Sahirs and the Order.

The covenants of Inverneira and Ipagrum are destroyed during the Sahirs’ retaliation, and most surviving covenants suffer major damage.

907 Normandy: Florum covenant founded in Flanders.

*This covenant originally occupied the site now inhabited by the magi of Spider’s Palace.*

907 Rome: Calabria covenant disbands in Rome, citing unbearable Roman politics. The magi apparently scatter, or possibly move to the Theban Tribunal.

*Calabria supposedly fled to Novgorod, but that Tribunal did not yet exist.*

C. 908 Iberia. Briatrix ex Diedne, of Duresca, calls for an emergency Tribunal to distribute aid to the covenants affected by the attacks of the sahirs. It is discovered that the Tribunal’s treasury is empty, as praeco Baldiccione spent it all on buying peace with the sahirs. Enraged by the deception and the death of her apprentice during the attacks, Briatrix kills Baldiccione in Wizard’s War.

911 Provencal: The Diedne covenant of Yf (821-911), off the coast of Arelat, falls to the depredations of Fraxinetum.

*One of its members joined the efforts of Coenobium Rhodanien in revenge but her line seems to fade away just before the Schism War.*

911 Rome: The Seventh Verdi Competition.

912 Transylvania: Tremere conference at Coeris.

913 Iberian Tribunal: Gamila ex Jerbiton is elected Praeco. Her influence popularizes Umayyad culture among the magi of the Tribunal.

The dissolution of Campus Stellae is announced. Bayt al-Hikma is established.

913 Normandy: Ligurio covenant founded, as a vassal of Fudarus on the Aquitaine-Brittany border in the Vendeé.

*It was destroyed by giants hurling huge boulders in 1011, presumed to have been sent by the Diedne covenant of Kerguntuil.*

913 Thebes: Local Tribunal meeting approves new Bjornaer covenant of Estropi.

914 Thebes: Estropi covenant founded on Mount Erciyes, the highest mountain in Anatolia by a sept of Bjornaer magi, all with heartbeasts of predatory birds. 

915 Iberia: The marriage of Conciatta ex Bonisagus and Currito ex Jerbiton declare the small Covenant of La Torre No Vista with the objective of advancing the Bonisagi's research.

The fields of Iberia fail, causing widespread famine.

915 Stonehenge: Covenant Lux Draconis founded in the Pennines.

917 Iberia: Duresca covenant founded in Iberia.

919 Iberia: The Iberian sahirs are officially expelled from the Levantine Suhhar after many years of little contact. 

There is a second famine in Iberia.

920 Iberia: Tamis filius Baldiccione founds the covenant of Pico Sacro, near the ancient Campus Stellae. 

Salama is founded by Bjornaer expelled from Mare Iacum.

The covenant of 'Olam wa-'ed is founded near the Jewish settlement of Lucena.

920 Normandy: Kerguntuil covenant founded, vassal of Branugurix. A coastal covenant situated on the Rose Coast. The members of Kerguntuil were particularly interested in the many Breton tales regarding giants, and many of their covenfolk were larger than average humans.
