*Destroyed in the Schism War 1007. It is rumored that a Quaesitor suggested to the Templars that they built their chapel at Lanleff to cleanse the region from the diabolic taint of the Diedne*

922 Transylvania: Tremere conference at Coeris.

923 Iberia: The maga Conciatta of Bonisagus publishes her first notable work, "The Application of Vim to the Supernatural Humors". This major breakthrough shows how Magic Theory can adapt and differentiate the four identifiable auras and earns her the rank of Cannophori. However, her experiments leave her Gift damaged.

*This was revolutionary in two ways. Firstly, it reduced auras into four categories that matched directly with the accepted Realms. Secondly, it demonstrated the use of Vim to directly affect those supernatural Realms. The House was at first skeptical, but as each of her peers studied from her work and validated her results, word of her achievement spread. Before long, the text was distributed to the Tribunals and Conciatta’s place in Hermetic history was sealed.*

*However, her parens Baltasar was Marched shortly after this, by his own admission he had become Infernally corrupted and he threw himself at the mercy of the Quaesitores in the hope for leniency but receiving none. The scandal hit Conciatta hard and her rivals had new ammunition, and she was quietly ostracized from Bonisagus society until she withdrew from the order in 937 and retreated into her research for many decades until her passing into Final Twilight in 1007 (see entry that year).*

*Note: Other sources list the publication year as 930.*

924 Iceland: Massive eruptions and earthquakes rock the land, and the skies darken while seeresses and soothsayers predict imminent destruction.

924 Stonehenge: King Edward dies, but he has brought all of England south of the Humber under his direct control, handing it over to Athelstan. The Vikings keep their established kingdom centered on York, and do not recognize British authority at all.

925 Balthasar ex Bonisagus, Conciatta's pater, is executed for diabolism.

925 Iberia: The Sahirs of Iberia formally join the Order of Hermes.

*In the Umayyad Emirate of the Iberian Peninsula, the sahirs who had caused the death of Flambeau’s pater had been persecuted by the Founder under his belief that they were diabolists. His followers had continued the witch-hunt in revenge for Flambeau’s death (which may not necessarily have been the fault of the sahirs). The Iberian sahirs saw this as an opportunity to protect themselves from their aggressors. Upon hearing of the inclusion in the Order of their hated enemy, House Flambeau was incensed. They promised a campaign of Wizard’s Wars until the last of these infidels had been exterminated. Nevertheless, the Iberian sahirs were few in number, and were able to hide deep in the Umayyad Emirate (and its successor, the Almohad Caliphate of Cordoba) until the fury of House Flambeau had abated somewhat.*

*One of the conditions of their joining was that no covenants would be founded in Mythic Africa for three centuries. The reason for this stipulation is not entirely understood. The new Hermetic sahirs may have been hiding from the jinn they had betrayed in joining the Order, or else it may have been imposed by the Hermetic negotiators to prevent the Order of Hermes from inheriting the settuten as enemies. The agreement expires in 1225, and at least some magi are eager to explore the new lands and the wonders therein.*

*Most of the sahirs join House Ex Miscellanea over the next 5 years.*

927 Iberia: The sahir fortresses of al-Abraj, Zaloquia and Jibal al-Rahma are reorganized as covenants.

927 Stonehenge: Athelstan conquers the kingdom of York.

927 Thebes: A famine rolls across the Byzantine Empire.

929 Iberia: Abd-Rahman III declares himself Caliph.

929 Rome: The Eighth Verdi Competition.

930 Iberia: Ruiz Sanchez ex Flambeau destroys the newly constituted sahir covenant of al-Abraj.

930 Provencal: The covenant of Barbegal (895–930), a watermill complex dominated by a trio of mage-smiths, this Verditius covenant just north of Arles was abandoned due to Saracen raiding of the undefended site. The surviving magi helped establish the Rhone based covenant of Coenobium Rhodanien. 

930 Rhine: Covenant Sirmium (822–930), located in the March of Meissen, is destroyed during the Magyar invasion of Germany.

*Only Wiederich of House Flambeau survived. Wiederich immediately joined Henry’s army, determined to avenge himself upon the horseman invaders. It is known that the Magyars brought wizards with them, but whether Wiederich was responsible for killing them is not clear; nevertheless, they were not present when Henry defeated the Magyars at Riade. Wiederich was also part of Otto I’s army, and present at the Battle of the Lech in 955, after which the Magyar leaders were executed and the assimilation of the Magyars into western Christendom began.*

930 Loch Leglean: Loch Leglean echoes with rumor of Damhan-Allaidh’s return. They prove baseless.

931 Stonehenge: King Athelstan completes his fathers’ and grandfathers’ work by accepting the tribute of the Welsh kings, and finally pacifying Cornwall. Covenant Sursum got too closely involved with the Cornish nobility and was connected to their revolt. Athelstan’s response involved the destruction of the covenant, and when the Quaesitors investigated the ruins they found evidence that the Tytalus at the covenant had been involved in diabolism.

*This evidence was some of the earliest against House Tytalus.*

**931 Eight Grand Tribunal: It is ratified that no Hermetic covenants may be established in Africa for three centuries, counted from the year 925 when Sahirs of the Umayyad Emirate were accepted to the Order.**

**Magus Hernis of House Tytalus cast out and executed by magus Fax Ignis of Flambeau, for abusing the right of Wizard’s War in declaring three such in the space of fourteen months. His reasons were considered insufficient by the Tribunal at Durenmar, but he refused to consider the advice of his sodales. Such abuse of the traditions of the Order shall not again be tolerated.**

Primus Quendalon of Merinita has not been seen much, and does not attend himself but sends a delegate.

*Hernis would have evaded this fate had he cooperated, but stubbornly refused to admit error or cooperate.*

931 Stonehenge: Covenant Sursum falls to a mortal king, Athelstan, for interfering in a mundane revolt. 

932 Transylvania: Tremere conference at Coeris.

934 Iberia: The covenant of Serra da Lousa is established.

Harra is founded as a peace project between sahirs and Flambeau.

934 Rhine Tribunal: The covenant of Oakdell is founded by magi of House Diedne in the Westerwald, just to the east of the Rhine, with the sponsorship of magi of the First Tribunal.

*It is clarified that Oakdell is forbidden from infringing on the territory of Rudiaria covenant.*

936 Normandy: Baiocassium Minor founded, a vassal of Baiocassium located within the town of Bayeux.

Destroyed in the Schism War 1005.

C. 937 Iberia: Briatrix ex Diedne discovers the documents that would eventually become known as the Duresca Scrolls while cataloging the spoils captured during her Wizard’s War with Baldiccione 29 years ago. The scrolls seem to be evidence of a secret plot by Founder Guernicus to dominate the Order and the World, whether they are authentic, or forgeries, is ambiguous. She secretly meets with some of the most influential magi at Tribunal, including Baldaccione's filius, Tamis. The latter confirms that the scrolls are written in his pater's handwriting, and, while Briatrix and the Redcap Arnau distribute copies throughout the Order, Tamis goes to the Inner Council of Magvillus seeking explanations.

The Guernicus is soon censured by the Inner Council, and he and Briatrix meet early deaths at the Battle of Frias. 

Eight Quaesitors, two of Guernicus, others of Flambeau, Tytalus, and Jerbiton, are dispatched to investigate the Duresca Scrolls. Three die en route, causes unrecorded. The covenant is paralyzed during the investigation.

*Shocking I must say! The details of this plot are either lost, unrecorded, or carefully kept under wraps. *

937 Iberia: Conciatta presents her last works and retires from the Order.

937 Stonehenge: King Athelstan is attacked by the Irish and Scots, in a grand alliance intended to break his power. He annihilates them at the battle of Brunanburh.

938 Stonehenge: Rector Novus founded on Isle of Man. Destroyed within the year, presumably by the Order of Odin. The second failure to colonize Man. *Alternately, this could be an early strike by Diedne. *

939 Stonehenge: King Athelstan dies, but his brother Edmund is unable to hold the kingdom together, and it quickly collapses under the pressure of Viking raids.

*He should not have alienated Hermetic allies. The various parts of the country*

*Slips away from the descendants of Alfred, until Cnut conquers them in 1016.*

940 Iberia: The panel of Quaesitors assigned to Duresca declare the Duresca Scrolls to be a fraud. This panel stays at Duresca and join the covenant, eventually fully rewriting its charter.

*Wait a minute..?*

941 Iberian Tribunal: Gamila ex Jerbiton loses his position as Praeco, as his support for the Quaesitorial actions in Duresca is considered an act of tyranny. Abbas ibn Mundir Trianomae is elected Praeco, and his newly founded covenant of Al-Zahra grows rapidly.

Tribunal records are shifted to Duresca.

941 Greater Alps: Probable year in which magus Nerius, patron saint of Christian magi, passes his Gauntlet to enter House Criamon.

*He goes on to be a most controversial and storied figure. Magus Nerius supposedly finished his apprenticeship in only five years.*

941 Normandy: Montverte covenant founded.

942 Thebes: Expedition by Michael of Amastris to found a covenant in Kiev.

942 Stonehenge: Contact lost with London covenant, which had moved in the city repeatedly. Presumed to be dispersed or destroyed.

942 Transylvania: Tremere conference at Coeris.

943 Normandy: Ergotism kills tens of thousands and leaves many more mad from visions. 

*This may have been a faerie joke gone too far or the effort of some vicious magus.*

946 Stonehenge: Covenant Nova Roma founded in Kent. The strongly Latin magi quickly acquire a poor reputation among Bjornaer, Diedne, and Ex Miscellanea magi.

*How they make enemies so quickly is not noted, although it was most probably from generally poor manners.*

947 Rome: The Ninth Verdi Competition.

948 Iberian Tribunal: The diffusion or allusion to the Duresca Manuscripts is declared a Minor Crime. Bayt al-Hikma declares its opposition to this measure and its commitment to a more open Order, allowing free access to its library.

C. 950: Casius invents the game Bellum (Latin for "War") as a method to practice Arcane Abilities and magical Arts for apprentices. During the game, the apprentice creates an illusion of a Roman soldier, which he then controls to fight an opponent’s illusionary soldier. To win, an apprentice must defeat his

opponent’s illusionary Roman. A second way to win is to force the opponent to lose concentration, which cancels the spell. Any number can play, the only requirements being that an apprentice’s Arts are opened, and he can cast the spell Ager Belli ("field of battle").

*This game soon became wildly popular in several Tribunals, and magi continued to play it past apprenticeship. It even ended in a minor riot at the Grand Tribunal of 1162.*

950 Iceland: Iceland’s magicians create a hreppur to protect their interests. 

951 Rome: Sansaron covenant founded in the ruins of Carthage.

952 Stonehenge: Nova Roma magi report contact with magi of London covenant, in a clash over a vis site. No further contact with London covenant reported. *London covenant had always been mysterious. Its fate remains a secret, but they left no one behind to care.*

952 Transylvania: Tremere conference at Coeris.

954 Loch Leglean: Caitlin Suil Uaine, half-sith maga aged 61, wins a faerie hill from the Prince of the Gray Jerkin in a shinty match. She founds the covenant of Crun Clach on the spot. Praeco Caitlin continues to head Crun Clach to this day.

*Really??*

955–973: The Slavs and Danes are converted to Christianity. New bishoprics are founded in the Eastern Marches and in Denmark. A number of pagan Slavs retreat to Rügen.

955 Thebes: Great Lavra founded on Mount Athos

956 Iberia: Al-Qurtubi ex Jerbiton completes his theory of alignment with the stars, which he compiles in the Ghayat al-Hakim, better known as Picatrix. He soon leaves the Order to devote himself to divine contemplation. The followers of his ideas are known as 'astral sorcerers'. 

956 Loch Leglean: Well of the Head sponsors Druids’ Dale, another covenant of Diedne.

956 Levant: Jahm of Alexandria, an Ex Miscellanea magus, rediscovers the basic principles of alchemical science and is the first to adapt them to Hermetic usage (i.e. Hermetic Alchemy) in a series of magical breakthroughs that also results in the discovery of Philosophic Alchemy.

*It received little attention in the Order until, in 1144, Roger of Chester published De Compositione Alchemiae, the first Latin work to introduce the ideas of Arabic alchemy to the wider European community.*

957: Regional Tribunals.

961 Iberia: Al-Hakam II is declared Caliph; during his reign, Cordoba becomes the cultural capital of the Mediterranean.

**961 Corruption of House Tytalus Special Tribunal: Prima Tasgillia Ex Tytalus and two other leaders of the House, and several members of Tytalus are tried, convicted, and executed for Diabolism. This marks a turning point in the Order’s relations with Hell and a strict anti-diabolic interpretation of the Code. House Tytalus is severely reduced in numbers. The diabolists also drag down many members of other Houses, having corrupted them. Within House Tytalus, it is known as the Betrayal as it was exposed by Tasgillia’s filia Kalliste when she brought evidence to the Quaesitors that her mater had been using Guorna’s lore to summon demons to attend to her sybaritic desires, opening the way for her to succeed Tasgillia as the fifth Prima and creating considerable ill will towards her in the House to this day.**

*This event, more than any other, triggers a decline of the Order and an increase in intra-Order conflict, leading to the Schism War. The execution of Tasgillia by Archmage Erythravis of Guernicus followed almost thirty years of investigation by Quaesitors. Many other magi were exposed as diabolists: the other House that suffered greatly in this purge was House Bonisagus, some members led into diabolism through spells designed by fallen Tytalus magi.*

*The Tytalan way would have been to settle the matter behind closed doors; instead the proud House was publicly shamed before the Order, and to this day has not recovered its former reputation. *

*Kalliste’s memory is ritually cursed every midwinter at Fudarus.*

Golias, a famous redcap of House Mercere and semi-permanent resident of Fudarus, was also convicted of diabolism and all known copies of his works were burned. His reputation for enjoying wine and women survived, even if he did not, and many years later a small group of artistic magi, Redcaps and their followers revived his name. They call him "Bishop Golias," and consider him a sort of patron saint of their group, the Goliards, or more formally the Ordo Vagarum ("The Wanderers").

*Their heyday is passing, for while they produced many great works in the late 1100s and early 1200s, including the famous Carmina Burana, the Church now preaches against them at services and strips suspected Goliards of church rank. They still continue to preach the Goliardic gospel of enlightened debauchery in Germany and northern France. This really should be intolerable behavior for Redcaps.*

962: The imperial coronation of Otto I (The Great) in Rome serves to legitimize his vast acquisitions. The German kings, henceforth are surnamed Emperor, and become inextricably entangled in the politics of Italy.

*Otto inherited the Duchy of Saxony and the kingship of the Germans upon his father's death in 936. He continued his father's work of unifying all German tribes into a single kingdom and greatly expanded the king's powers at the expense of the aristocracy. After defeating the Magyars at the Battle of Lechfeld in 955, thus ending the Hungarian invasions of Western Europe, he earned a reputation as the savior of Christendom.*

962 Rhine Tribunal: The Tribunal has heard charges of diabolism brought against Eginolf of House Flambeau of Rudiaria covenant, which remain unproven. Reports of malefic activity in the vicinity of Rudiaria are, however, noted with disapproval, and that covenant is therefore ordered to investigate and report within seven years. The magi of Rudiaria are also urged to redouble their efforts to avoid disturbing the mundanes of the region.

962 Thebes: Michael of Amastris, founder of a covenant in Kiev, retreats to Constantinople, fleeing local hedge wizards called "Volkhvy". Four other magi of the covenant have been slain by the various dangers of Russia.

962 Transylvania: Tremere conference at Coeris.

963: Regional Tribunals are a year early due to the Grand Tribunal.

963 Iberia: The magi of Bayt al-Hikma belonging to the Kharijite movement are executed by order of the Caliph, for their dissemination of heretical ideas. Praeco Abbas ibn Mundir convinces the Tribunal not to intervene.

963 Thebes: Redcap Methodius rejuvenates covenant of Miphoria

**964 Ninth Grand Tribunal. The "unwieldy" Theban Tribunal is split in two - the Tremere dominated north becomes the Transylvanian Tribunal, the southern part the Thebes Tribunal.**

Primus Quendalon of Merinita has not been seen much, and does not attend himself but sends a delegate.

*No other important rulings noted. It may have been poorly attended in the wake of the Corruption.*

965 Rome: The Tenth Verdi Competition.

969 Iberia: The Umayyad Caliphate and the Order reach an agreement, allowing magi with the Gentle Gift to teach and be taught in the Great Mosque of Cordoba.

969 Levant: The Fatimid Caliphate under general Jawhar captures Egypt, supported by Berber wizards who practice a form of magic never encountered before by other Muslim wizards. The mages who oppose the invasion are utterly destroyed.

*No magi of the Order ever had contact with them, so the magic they wielded remains a mystery, although the evidence would seem to suggest that it was of great power.*

969 Rhine Tribunal: House Mercere reports that covenant Rudiaria (892-969), affiliated to House Flambeau and located in the Rhine Gorge, is destroyed - burnt down in an apparent catastrophic magical accident, with all of its magi slain or missing. The exact cause is not revealed. It is noted that this event coincided with a crippling famine in the region, which has contributed to mundane unrest. Declarations of regret are heard for the believed loss of these magi.

*This covenant suffered from malefic attention, and demonic involvement was suspected in the magical accident that destroyed the covenant.*

970 Provencal: The Birds of Camargue (901-970), a Bjornaer covenant in the swamp at the mouth of the Rhone merge with the greater grouping of Coenobium Rhodanien in response to the threat of Fraxinetum. 

970 Thebes: John Tzimices allows Paulician heretics to settle in the city of Philippolis to help defend it.

971 Thebes: Kievan Rus invades Bulgaria and threatens Constantinople.

972 Transylvania: Tremere conference at Coeris.

975 Provencal: The covenant of Fraxinetum (889-975), founded by a band 

of Andalusian raiders and two aggressive Tytalus magi that provoked a series of disputes regarding the Peripheral Code and interference with mundanes, is finally destroyed soon after the defeat of the Saracens (in 973) in a Wizard’s War declared by the newly formed covenant of Coenobium Rhodanien.

*This alliance of Arelat covenants banded together to resist the incursions and remain a potent force in the region to this day.  *

976 Iberian Tribunal: The Tribunal approves a Mercere expedition to Africa, breaking the spirit, but not the letter, of the agreement with the Sahirs.

976 Rhine Tribunal: House Mercere reports that nothing is salvageable from the former covenant of Rudiaria, and that the site has been sanctified by a local archbishop from Mainz. Oakdell covenant’s claim of ownership of all sites formerly belonging to Rudiaria is refused.

977 Loch Leglean: Giants destroy covenant of Uamh na Croig. Investigation shows that the magi of Uamh na Croig had hunted giants for sport, and no retribution is made. The magi of Loch Leglean are warned to leave the giants alone.

*Surely these magi paid no attention to the history of the war against Damhan-Allaidh. Learn from their example, magus!*

978 Iberia: Estancia-es-Karida covenant founded.

980 Iberia. Al-Manṣūr becomes absolute dictator of the Umayyad empire, gets rid of the Syrian aŷnād and make the Berbers his personal troops.

980 Hibernia: Teamhrach was threatened by Olaf Cuaran, king of Dublin and Northumberland. Unwilling to see Teamhrach fall into the hands of northmen, the covenant of Vigil magically ensured a treaty between Máel Sechnaill mac Domnaill and the kings of Leinster and Ulster. In further clear breach of the Code, the magi of Vigil also took to the field of battle when Máel Sechnaill’s forces met Olaf’s and helped rout the Scandinavians, which left the way clear for Máel Sechnaill to take Dublin.

*The Order of Hermes was always loath to involve itself in the mundane conflict between the Viking kingdoms and the native Irish kings unless the northmen brought rune wizards. *

980 Thebes: Grammaton covenant founded by Grammaton of Bonisagus and his filii. They were devoted to the completion of original research connected to binding magics.

981 Normandy: Caput Capra covenant founded, a vassal of Branugurix, and the furthest east of Normandy’s Diedne covenants, on the banks of the Loire not far from Fleury.

*Caput Capra was the youngest of Branugurix’s vassals, and was founded to protect the ancient druidic site at Fleury from the lawlessness of the late 10th century. Caput Capra was the first casualty of the Schism War 1005 in the Tribunal. *

982 Iberia: Izaro is abandoned

982 Transylvania: Tremere conference at Coeris.

983 Iberia: Aedes Iris is founded near Tafilalt, as a Mercer House. Its commercial importance allows the Order easy access to the gold and vis of Bilad al-Sudan.

983 Hibernia Tribunal: The clear interference in mundane affairs by the covenant of Vigil at the Battle of Teamhrach in 980 was not punished as it was deemed vital for the sanctity of the Tribunal.

983 Normandy Tribunal (17th): In light of the preeminence of the Duke of Normandy in the territories claimed by the Tribunal, and the decline of the Franks as a people, the Tribunal supports the proposal of Adrianus of Montverte, and the Tribunal of the West Franks is renamed the Normandy Tribunal.

983 Rome: The Eleventh Verdi Competition.

984 Stonehenge: Ungulus covenant founded in the Lake District by members of House Flambeau.

986 Iberia: Occelum Durii is sacked by the troops of Almanzor.

C. 987 Loch Leglean: Clan Mac Gruagach joins House Ex Miscellanea. Covenant leader Callum Mac Lachlan begins sending his clansmen among the magi of the Order.

*Evidently, Clan Mac Gruagach had not joined the Order along with the rest of the Ordo.*

987 Provencal: Calebais covenant founded.

*The original founders of Calebais were punished by Tribunal for having aided rebels against the king, and it was in honor of their outrageous act that the covenant had adopted the symbol of a broken crown.*

990 Iberia: Porta Pandere is founded near Santarém (Portugal) as a complement to the work of Aedes Iris.

990 Rhine: Followers of Hercynius leave Durenmar to found Waldherz.

*First a chapter house, later a (disputed) covenant.*

C. 990–1002: Long held tensions between the Latinate and non-Latinate Houses (Diedne, Ex Miscellanea, and Bjornaer) keep mounting.

*Some covenants, especially in The British Isles and in Normandy, seriously start preparing for the worst. Vis is stockpiled rather than used. Magic discoveries and secrets are held onto and not shared - not even by Bonisagii. *

992 Thebes: Rodnya Covenant founded in Kiev, sponsored by the Theban Tribunal.

*This is the first colonization attempt since Michael of Amastris failed.*

992: Legend claims that in this year a Northman, Leif the Lucky, son of Eric the Red, journeyed to the lands west of Greenland.

*These lands must be an unknown regio, perhaps a Faerie kingdom?*

992 Transylvania: Tremere conference at Coeris.

993 Rhine: Grand Silesia Covenant founded in Poland, sponsored by magi from Crintera and Rethra of the Rhine Tribunal. The easternmost expansion of the Order to date.

994 Transylvania: Pripet Maior Covenant founded on the south-western edge of the Pripet marshes, sponsored by the Transylvanian Tribunal.

996: Most Regional Tribunals are a year early due to the Grand Tribunal.

996 Iberian Tribunal: Praeco Abbas ibn Mundir is declared guilty of Interference with the mundanes, having conspired with the Caliph during the execution of the Khariji magi. However, he is able to flee.

Ibn Sahl ex Tremere is elected Praeco. He immediately begins legal action against Mons Vindius, the main Diedne covenant of the Tribunal.

996 Rhine Tribunal: Waldherz, deep in the Black Forest, was originally founded as a chapter house of Durenmar in 990 by a handful of Bonisagus magi who constituted the lineage of Hercynius. He was the penultimate apprentice of Bonisagus, and his followers claimed to continue what they believed to be Bonisagus’ final work, which was to overcome the limits on Hermetic magic using primal magic from the forest’s heart. They were joined in this endeavor by

magi of House Diedne, and Waldherz declared its independence from Durenmar, although the issue of their sponsorship to form a separate covenant was mired in controversy. Durenmar argued that they could veto them by providing no sponsor; Waldherz claimed that one of their magi sufficed as a sponsor, since they had not yet seceded. The Tribunal ruled in favor of Durenmar, clarifying that a sponsor cannot be one of the founders of a new covenant.

Oakdell covenant brings charges of espionage and of meddling in their affairs against magi of Durenmar covenant. The claims are unproven.

997 Iberian Tribunal: Magus Pistulus of Flambeau punished with the loss of his familiar after slaying magus Forcus of Tytalus. Pistulus believed Forcus to be threatening his laboratory and apprentice, but presented no good evidence of this intent.

*This is one of the few Tribunals to occur in the same year as a Grand Tribunal.*

**997 Tenth Grand Tribunal: After the episode with Pistulus of Flambeau, the Tribunal rules that every sanctum shall be marked with a circle within a square, with straight lines connecting the square’s opposite corners, with a symbol of the owner accompanying the marker.**

The assembled Merinitae finally decide they have to choose one of their number to take Quendalon’s place, but they do not all agree that he’s dead. By surprising consensus the leaders of the various groups name a timid young maga named Rhiannon as Prima; she was four generations descended from Quendalon, and they believed she would best represent the House’s interests.

*At this point, no one could really say what had become of Quendalon, and he hadn’t been seen for many decades. If still alive, he would have been ancient.*

The Diedne covenant of Oakdell in the Rhine again brings charges of espionage and of meddling in their affairs against magi of Durenmar covenant. These spurious claims are unproven.

998 Normandy: Aedes Salii covenant, vassal of Sinapis, established on Jersey. This covenant - named after the Salii, the priests of Mars - was founded to coordinate the Schism War in Normandy and Stonehenge.

*After the war, the Tremere members returned to their home Tribunals, leaving it in the hands of House Flambeau. It was disbanded in 1063 when House Flambeau withdrew from the Normandy Tribunal; two of its members joined Lapis Crudus.*

998 Stonehenge: Lumen covenant founded.

999–1003: Papacy of Gerbert of Aurillac; he reigns as Sylvester II. He is rumored to be a powerful magician, and to have had the advice of an animated bronze head, possibly the one that advised Vergil. 

*Was he a hedge mage or diabolist?*

999: At the Bjornaer Gathering of Twelve Years at Crintera, concerns are raised that the Order of Hermes is in danger of fragmenting due to internal conflict. *The non-Latinate Houses of Diedne, Ex Miscellanea, and Bjornaer seemed to be in particular danger, as the traditionally Roman Houses banded together and openly attacked magi from "primitive" traditions in flagrant disregard of the Oath of Hermes. House Bjornaer became paranoid and insular, and many retreated to Crintera over the coming years. *

C. 1000: Some chroniclers report a great and widespread fear that the world is about to end. The churches are packed with the repentant and many give up their worldly goods to the Church.

*They were proved wrong, but what may have caused this?*

C. 1000 Iberia: Hafsa ibn Abbas Trianomae joins Dar Maliha and initiates a major improvement of the covenant, both in its infrastructure and political contacts.

1000 Iberia: The Qadis of Cordoba condemn the covenant of al-Zahra and its members flee.

1001 Rome: Literatus covenant founded with a grant from Pope Sylvester II. The relationship between this pope and the Order is comparatively friendly.

1001 Rome: The Twelfth Verdi Competition.

1002 Rhine: On the eve of the Schism War, Hérisson, Bjornaer of Clan Arelie, is slain by Gypaetus of Bjornaer who declared Wizard’s War against him for betraying cultic secrets to magi hostile to the Order. Hérisson’s passion was for a process he called "Hermetic Viresculture", the cultivation of vis. At odds with his Primus and many of his House he believed that Magic was infinite and as an advocate of peace, he had offered copies of his research to House Bonisagus and explained his theories to convocations of House Diedne.  

*The legend of Hérisson’s lost garden is widespread in the Order; supposedly this is where the magus conducted his research into the production of vis. This was never taken seriously though. Some claim that he was a descendant of Myanar of House Merinita who fled when Quendalon became Primus.*

1002 Transylvania: Tremere conference at Coeris.

*This meeting immediately precedes the chaos of the Schism War. Coincidence?*

**1003–1012 The Schism War: The early stages of the war are simply chaos, with no known trigger. It was growing mainly out of mistrust from the Latin Houses of the Order towards the non-Christian Houses, and particularly there remained centuries-old mistrust against the Druidic House of Diedne, who were also rumored to be practicing human sacrifice. Many magi hoped the regional Tribunals of 1004 would help restore order, but individual Wizard’s Wars and impromptu Marches forced cancellations of most of the Regional Tribunal meetings. Magi battles magi, assassinations are performed, covenants turn on themselves, forests burned, plains wasted, and cities leveled in the war between Houses and Covenants.**

**The war reached a point of no return with the declaration of Wizard’s War by Primus Cercistum of House Tremere on the entire House Diedne in 1010 - accusing them of human sacrifice and diabolism. House Flambeau enthusiastically allies with Tremere against Diedne. House Guernicus ratifies the declaration and the Order decides - with a narrow majority vote - to renounce House Diedne entirely at the emergency Grand Tribunal of 1010 at Magvillus.**

*The records are confused in this period. Most chroniclers agree that the Schism War began as general chaos, culminating in the war between Tremere and Diedne, and that most of the events took place from 1003 to 1012.*

*Diedne had been mistrusted for years for its secretive ways and adherence to alien magic. If they had only opened their closed doors, in the tradition of the Order, they might have avoided their fate. \
Half the magi of House Tremere died during the War, but the conflict went a long way towards restoring the pride that they had lost due to the Sundering and the intrigue it exposed. *

*Before the Schism War was fully resolved, Prima Kalliste of Tytalus, who was widely mistrusted in her own House and perceived to be following the lead of House Guernicus meekly, was murdered by a filius of her successor, Klynoites the sixth Prima.*

*Primus Aldico of Mercere works hard in this period to restore peace, but he seems to have been more successful after the Tempest (1212). *

*House Guernicus, headed by Primus Antonius, attempted a peaceful solution but ended up assaulting House Diedne among terrible internal conflict. However, the conflict was generally for the House's benefit in that it restored the perception of importance of the Quaesitores.*

*House Ex Miscellanea was headed by Primus Basilicus, who was to participate in the fateful Grand Tribunal carrying a considerable number of proxy sigils that might have turned the tide of decision. Unfortunately, he never arrived, at least not in able time.*

*House Flambeau, headed by Primus Entisimon, generally approved of the War and enabled it by having many among its ranks decree Wizard Wars against members of House Diedne. Eventually, Primus Entisimon rallied the whole House against House Diedne with his famous speech at Val-Negra.*

*House Jerbiton largely kept apart, choosing instead to help in the period of reconstruction. Many of its members individually joined the fray though.*

*House Bonisagus was terribly divided by the conflict, widening the rift between the Trianoma and Bonisagus factions, and leading to the abdication of Primus Thamus Collis after the renunciation of House Diedne.*

*House Mercere was already well-established in its role of running communications within the Order of Hermes, and was instrumental in the calling of the Grand Tribunal.*

*House Bjornaer, being non-Roman itself, decided early on to retreat into Crintera for well over a decade and ended up largely avoiding the whole conflict.*

*House Verditius remained unattached and uninvolved with any accusations or hostilities during the whole period.*

*House Merinita kept some degree of sympathy for House Diedne during the whole period, even after the Renunciation, which was opposed by Prima Rhiannon.*

1003 Stonehenge: Lumen covenant destroyed by a Diedne attack after only five years of existence.

1004 Iberia: Maslama al-Majriti founds a school of mathematics and astrology in Cordoba.

1004 Rhine Tribunal: Restas of Jerbiton has used magic to aid him in negotiating prices for books, thereby acquiring a substantial library at little cost. He was found guilty of interfering with mortals, as book dealers would be wary of those seeking books and might bring suspicion on others who sought to buy them. He is punished by the loss of his library.

A disputed ruling exile all of its members of the Rowan Gild. Flambeau and Tremere magi of the Ash Gild, in collaboration with some of the Oak Gild, declare Wizard War against the Diedne magi of Waldherz and Oakdell.

*About fifty years previously, all of the Diedne magi in the Tribunal had split from*

*the Hawthorn Gild to form the Rowan Gild. They were later joined by the Bonisagus magi of Waldherz and some Bjornaer magi from Grand Silesia. This new faction was already perceived to be excessively secretive and soon attracted*

*many enemies in the Tribunal.*

*This was the first outbreak of the Schism War in the Rhine Tribunal, and anarchy and violence soon spread throughout the land.*

*Severus Maximus of House Flambeau declares Wizard War against Caithlinn of House Diedne (of Oakdell covenant). Magus Cirion of Tremere declares Wizard War against Pietro of House Diedne (of Oakdell covenant).*

1004 Thebes: The 20th Theban Tribunal recognized the covenant of Diadelphia on the island of Kalymnos, founded by two brothers. Cosmas and Baztas, of House Verditius.

*Other Verditius magi joined the covenant, but left due to the bitter rivalry between the brothers. *

C. 1004: Quaesitor Patrick of Dunderry, of Rodnya covenant, refuses orders to attack a covenant in the Carpathians. He leads first Rodnya, then the other Slavic covenants, on a peaceful path during the Schism War.

*Q. Patrick is not known to have suffered any direct punishment for his rebellion.*

1005 Normandy: Caput Capra covenant on the banks of Loire becomes the first casualty of the Schism War in the Tribunal.

*Apprentices of Confluensis are still brought to the scant ruins of the covenant to receive their lessons in history.*

Baiocassium Minor covenant is also destroyed.

1006: Himinis the Mad of House Verditius begins to make cursed items. His devices contain useful powers, but also include dangerous effects that he considers humorous.

*Himinis produced and sold many magical devices over the next several decades, enhancing his avaricious nature and making him one of the most greedy Verditius magi to have ever lived. He became paranoid that other magi were trying to steal his items, so he started creating trick devices, items that discharged dangerous spells at the wielder when triggered. Not satisfied with this chicanery, Himinis invented a Mystery that allowed him to instill powerful curses into his items.*

*These items continue to surface centuries after his death in 1047.*

1006 (Autumn): The three Slavic covenants reconcile and unite against outsiders. These three covenants had previously been at odds over issues of land and Tribunal authority.

1006: A new star is sighted in the heavens. It lasts for several years before eventually vanishing.

*An omen of things to come?*

C. 1006: The Quaesitors begin to investigate all the Houses for diabolism, ostensibly to follow up on the many accusations flying about, and to check after the Corruption of Tytalus. The Houses resist, none more vehemently than House Diedne. Diedne accuses House Tremere of using the Quaesitors to lay bare their magic secrets.

*It wouldn’t be surprising if Tremere used it as a convenient pretext.*

1007: The Order hosts the Second Secular (every 120 years).

*This year was officially canceled due to the strife within. As rumor has it, the Latin Houses still had it.*

1007 Iberia: Conciatta of Bonisagus enters Final Twilight. A brilliant researcher, but shunned by her House.

*Rumors are that she had found ways to overcome the deleterious effects that the Divine has on magic, but few believe that.*

*See 923, 930.*

1007 Normandy: Kerguntuil covenant on the Rose Coast is destroyed.

1008 Normandy: Pagus covenant, vassal of Confluensis, is destroyed.

1008 Provencal: The founding magi of covenant Donjon Leyre (955–1008), at the fork of the Leyre river in the Landes, all perish fighting in the Schism War. An early vassal of Montverte, the covenant was originally established as a forward base for raiding into the Basque-held Pyrenees. 

1008 Rhine: The Diedne dominated covenant Oakdell (931–1008) in the Westerwald (Thuringian Forest), near the Rhine Gorge, is destroyed in the Schism War.

*On numerous occasions in the 10th century they accused Durenmar of scrying, but these charges were never proven.*

1008: New Slavonic Tribunal: Slavic covenants break ties with their sponsors and declare themselves the New Slavonic Tribunal.

*Technically, the New Slavonic covenants did not form a full Tribunal, having only three member covenants. In 1053 it changes its name to the Novgorod Tribunal.*

1008 Stonehenge: Nova Roma destroyed by Diedne magi.

1008 Loch Leglean: The Schism War comes to Caledonia. Many magi Ex Miscellanea, particularly the gruagachan, join with Flambeau and Tremere magi against House Diedne. Holy Oak, Well of the Head, and Druids’ Dale, all Diedne covenants, destroyed. Diedne had made itself the enemy of Ex Miscellanea for years.

*This support of the efforts of Flambeau and Tremere is little remarked on in those houses.*

1009 Iberia: The Umayyad Caliphate fragments into several taifas.

1009 Nuinsil of Criamon begins his travels through Muslim lands and investigates the nature of Sufi teachings, the first of many such Criamon journeys in the Orient. Many of his House have done the same and brought back wisdom and strange philosophies.

*It seems likely that their travels were spurred by a desire to escape the chaos of the War.*

*The nature of the Sufi teachings seem to appeal to the enigmatic Criamon magi, and the Criamon seem to have (a bit too) much to offer the Muslims. While most of the Order knows little of Sufi power, some of the skills exhibited by its followers seem to duplicate Hermetic magic (were these perhaps taught by Criamon magi in exchange for foreign insights?). Tales told by the Sufis, and by others about the Sufi, imply that they may possess powers kept secret from the wider Order. Most of the Order, however, view them as crackpot hedge wizards, given to speaking in riddles. Maybe the truth of the Sufi's enigma lies in their riddles, and only Criamon magi can truly decipher the extent of Sufi power?*

1009 New Slavonic Tribunal: Rodnya covenant abandoned and destroyed by extra-Tribunal covenants from Thebes and Transylvania. The magi flee to the city of Novgorod.

*These magi later found Three Lakes covenant.*

1009 New Slavonic Tribunal: Covenant Grand Silesia (993–1009) is destroyed in the Schism War. Founded between the Oder and Warta rivers in Poland by magi from Rethra and Crintera, this covenant split from the Rhine Tribunal in 1008 to form the New Slavonic Tribunal. Refugees flee to Pripet Maior. 

*Rhine magi, feeling betrayed, destroyed the covenant under the guise of the Schism War. Grand Silesia is resettled in 1013.*

1009 Provencal: The spirit masters of covenant Campus Elysii (843–1009), dwelling in the Alyscamps, are driven out by the expansion of the Coenobium.

*Although the group retreated to the Verdun Gorge, attempting to refound their home under the new name of Portus Termini, the settlement failed before the next Tribunal and was never officially registered as a Provençal covenant.*

C. 1010: Descendants of the sorceress Gyongy, of the original tradition that sired Bjornaer, join House Bjornaer for protection from the Schism War.

*The Line of Gyongy still runs strong in House Bjornaer, and all of that Line have the same heart-beast, a magpie.*

1010: Primus Cercistum of House Tremere declares Wizard’s War on the entire House Diedne. House Flambeau enthusiastically allies with Tremere. Many magi from various houses, especially Jerbiton, follow soon after. House Guernicus calls for an Emergency Tribunal, where a majority vote is cast to renounce House Diedne and all her members for refusal of Quaesitorial investigation.

*Primus Entisimon of Flambeau rallies his followers with an enflaming speech that remains stirring in the reading.*

1010 Iberia: Assault on Mons Vindius, the main Diedne covenant of Iberia, by a coalition of Tremere, Flambeau and Jerbiton. The Flambeau of Brigantia position themselves in favor of the Diedne.

*Pico Sacro is destroyed by an elder dragon.*

1010: The inner council of Magvillus, led by Primus Antonius, summons thirty Guernicus magi to the domus magna to perform the "Curse of Thoth" greater ritual against House Diedne. The end of the ritual (*a sacrifice?*) was done half a Diameter after sunrise; thus at least some Diedne were caught without Parma. Thirty-nine pawns of vis were consumed by the ritual.

*All but the most senior Vim specialists of House Diedne should have been affected - if the ritual had any effectiveness at all. There are evil rumors that the Diedne representative under Quaesitorial protection was actually sacrificed in the ritual. If this is really true, the hypocrisy is absolutely staggering!*

1010 Rhine: Covenant Waldherz (990–1010) located in the Black Forest and dedicated to the teaching of Hercynius (the penultimate apprentice of Bonisagus), is lost.

*This covenant was never officially sanctioned by the Rhine Tribunal due to the veto of Durenmar.*

*Hercynius followers claimed to continue what they believed to be Bonisagus’ final work, which was to overcome the limits on Hermetic magic using primal magic from the forest’s heart.*

1011: Most Tribunal meetings this year are canceled due to the war.

1011 Normandy: Baiocassium covenant, vassal of Branugurix, and predominantly House Diedne is destroyed.

Ligurio covenant, vassal of Fudarus, destroyed by giants hurling huge boulders.

*Presumed to have been sent by the Diedne covenant of Kerguntuil.*

1011 Provencal: The covenant of Askelarre, founded by a coven of Hermetic sorginak (Basque witches) in 945 is destroyed in the Schism war.

1011 Stonehenge: The now Nameless Covenant, in Anglesey, is destroyed in one of the last major conflicts of the Schism War.

*Quaesitors investigating the covenant’s remains find something or some things in the ruins that lead them to obliterate the site entirely and destroy most of its records.*

1012 Iberia: The southern covenants of the Tribunal declare their independence, calling themselves the 'Tribunal of Al-Andalus' and declaring their neutrality in the Schism War. Hafsa ex Bonisagus, of the covenant of Dar Maliha, is elected Praeco.

Porta Pandere and Aedes Iris decide to support the Andalusians, breaking ties with Aedes Saturni. The magi of Laminium, alien to the Order until then, join the newly formed Tribunal. Azm Alfil is constituted.

1012 New Slavonic Tribunal: Three Lakes covenant founded by Quaesitor Patrick of Dunderry and other former magi of Rodnya, in Novgorod.

1012 Provencal: The former residence of Flambeau the Founder and original domus magna of his House, Val-Negra (774–1012), is believed to have been destroyed in the last days of the Schism War.

*There is considerable mystery as to the reason for the original covenant’s abandonment, and even its exact location is now forgotten.*

*The original domus magna was a covenant on the Spanish side of the Pyrenees mountains. It seems reasonable to assume that during the Schism War, Primus Entisimon found that Val-Negra's remote location - originally chosen for its strategic value against the sahirs of Moorish Iberia - made it unsuitable as a headquarters in the Schism War against Diedne, so he moved his command to the covenant of Castra Solis. His successor officially moved the House's headquarters to the new site; there are also rumors that neoRoman elements within the House had a hand in the decision to move the domus magna.*

C. 1012: Iarna of Flambeau, a mighty maga, swears an oath to hunt down every last Diedne.

*She soon disappears in the midst of the Schism War. An Ex Miscellanea prophecy (now tradition) claims that if Diedne reappears, so will Iarna.*

1012 Transylvania: Tremere conference at Coeris.

*The records are unclear, but it seems likely that this meeting preceded the declaration of Wizard’s War by House Tremere on House Diedne.*

**1012 Normandy: Battle of the Tempest: A contingent of powerful Flambeau, Tremere, Tytalus, and Quaesitor magi engage the remnants of House Diedne in a cataclysmic final battle at Mount Dol. The Diedne unleash storms, summoned horrors, and the raw fury of ancient rites - but despite their fury, they are broken. Nearly all magi on both sides perish in a month-long struggle marked by blood, fire, and thunder.**

The entrances to the regio holding the covenant Branugurix, domus magna of House Diedne, are somehow closed by the Primus of the house during the climactic battle, and several Flambeau and Tremere magi are lost, trapped inside. The fate of the Diedne leadership remains shrouded: some say Primus Llewellyn and his inner circle vanished into Faerie, others that they fled across the seas to distant lands. Whispers persist that elements of House Ex Miscellanea gave them shelter - though many magi Ex Miscellanea fought fiercely against them, particularly in Loch Leglean, where kin-slaying and loyalty ran on parallel tracks.

*It is said that when the druids knew the end had come, they chose to make their deaths a curse - echoing the Cymric myths in which their ancestors summoned the sea to swallow their sacred isle, rather than see it fall to enemies.*

*The exact location and the former method of entering the regio is rumored to be detailed in the records of House Guernicus. It is said that House Guernicus takes a great interest in those who spend a lot of time in the area around Mount Dol.*

*The Tempest stands as the closing act of the Schism War - its final convulsion and bloody coda.*

After 1012: The Schism War ends with the destruction of House Diedne. Houses Tremere and Flambeau have both suffered losses of half their number; House Mercere has lost at least twelve Redcaps. Fighting declines, and ends by the end of three years. The emblem of House Diedne is struck from all texts and records of the Order.

*The heavy losses may incidentally be why certamen became the predominant means for settling disputes as those who had refused to change were mostly gone, and the Druidic and Mercurian traditions are no longer a force within the Order. However, some claim that Tremere or Tytalus gained access to ancient Diedne secrets - depending whom of them you ask. If true, the evils of Diedne may yet continue.*

C. 1013–1063 Normandy: Houses Tytalus and Flambeau bicker about the rich pickings of vis sources and magical sites left in the wake of the Schism War, especially in the traditional Tytalus territory of Brittany. It became as much about ideology as resources. House Tytalus accused House Flambeau of demanding to be rewarded for carrying out their self-appointed duties in slaying members of House Diedne. Scorn was poured on the Flambeaus’ self-righteous protestations of justice and honor; there was nothing honorable, the Tytali claimed, about grabbing resources from other Houses and claiming they were spoils of war. At Tribunal, Primus Klynoites referred to the Flambeau as narrow-minded fools who suborned their magic to the dictates of the Quaesitores rather than reveling in the power of their Gifts. The inter-House conflict threatened to return the Order to the pre-Schism lawlessness. Certamens over resources and territory escalated into cycles of vengeance driven Wizard’s Wars until even the original disputes were forgotten, at least by the Tytali.

1013 Iberia (North). Tremébriga is destroyed in a Diedne assault. The Praeco Ibn Sahl is killed in the attack.

A Civil war (the Fitna) breaks out in the Umayyad Caliphate. It lasts 18 years.

1013 New Slavonic Tribunal: Grand Silesia covenant resettled.

*In Poland the mid eleventh century saw the terrible civil war and pagan uprising.*

*Grand Silesia tried to stay out of sight during the conflict but there were too many people with grudges against them. Malicious tongues in the Rhine Tribunal may have wagged, for when Casimir the Restorer came back to power in 1039, the covenant was targeted and destroyed by crusading anti-pagan knights.*

1013 Thebes: The covenant of Aegaea founded by Orithyia of Merinita, supposedly in the palace of Poseidon at the bottom of the Aegean Sea. The covenant dedicates itself to the preservation and study of legends native to the Tribunal, both contemporary and ancient.

*Redcaps attest to the existence (and splendor) of the site and its patron. Since its founding, Aegaea has remained a poorly understood and somewhat withdrawn presence in the Tribunal, mistrusted by many. Apart from Redcaps, very few magi have ever seen the site, although most of those who have speak warmly of their reception. *

1014: A Bulgarian army is blinded by a Constantinopolitan general. House Tremere recruits some of the blind people, offering them their sight for their lifelong obedience.

1014 Hibernia: The Tribunal’s magi had thought Hibernia was free of Diedne, but they were proved wrong. Brian Bóramha’s druid, a dwarf named Muírcheartach mac Lía, tells the Flambeau magi at Lámbaird that Bóramha have druids with him who call themselves "sons of Diedne".  At the Battle of Clontarf on Good Friday, the Lámbaird Flambeau stalk the battlefield looking for the Diedne. The combat between the Flambeau and the Diedne is brief, flamboyant, and lethal, and spells the true end of House Diedne in Hibernia.

*The Irish defeated the Ostmen, who never threatened the Irish again, but Bóramha himself died in the battle.*

*No one is certain what happened to the corpses of the three Diedne magi who fell at Clontarf. Cliffheart covenant claims to have one as their cathach, even though they were not involved in the battle. Others say that Qui Sonant Pro Quieto buried the Diedne with their enemies, placing each in a grave with a Hermetic magus who would have been their rival. As far as is known, none were given Christian burials.*

1014 Iberia: Jaferiya covenant founded.

*The Covenant of Jaferiya is said to have been founded on the site of one of the villas that Flambeau's vulgar family lost to invading Muslims. From all accounts, it seems that the Seneca family was prosperous and happy throughout the Roman rule of Iberia.*

1014 Iberia: Jerbiton covenant of Barcelona founded. After the ruins of a Roman temple of Mercury were found within the city, two followers of Jerbiton quickly ensured title to the land. In the next four years the covenant repeatedly encounters the Jewish magicians called Kabbalists, who object to some of the magi's actions sparking retributions against the Jewish community, as local Christians were more than willing to blame any seemingly "diabolical" activity on the Jews.

*See 1018 regarding the Kabbalists.* *Though conflict initially flared, cooler heads prevail and the Kabbalists and Jerbiton magi live peacefully until representatives of House Flambeau become involved, culminating at the Tribunal of 1072.*

*Though the Covenant of Barcelona had a small membership (only eight magi at its most crowded) it began to build great influence within the Iberian Tribunal. The city of Barcelona had great resources of knowledge and had access to  the trade routes of the Mediterranean. Trading these resources for favors and cultivating close ties with Iberian Quaesitors, Barcelona earned political power that far outstripped its membership.*

1014 Rome: The independent covenant of Vardian's Tomb founded.

1014 Stonehenge: Blackthorn sponsors Tagelyn covenant in nearby caves, supposedly to begin repairing the social damage of the Schism War.

*Here begins one of the great betrayals by Blackthorn.*

1014–1018 Transylvania: The Byzantines invade Bulgaria. After defeating the Bulgarian army, the Byzantine leader blinds his 14,000 captives, save one whom he blinds only in a single eye so that he can lead his fellows back to the capital. King Samuel of the Bulgars dies of shock at the sight of the fumbling mass of men. Tremere magi offers sight back to many of these men, in exchange for

lifelong service to the House.

The kingdom is completely subdued within four years.

*The "coincidence" of timing was exquisitely unfortunate for the Tremere.*

*House Tremere accuse House Jerbiton and Theban covenants of magically supporting the attack, but cannot prove it. *

1015 Hibernia: Covenant of Cliffheart is founded by three Merinita magi after their covenant, Cosán Ceolaire ("Warbler’s Way") was destroyed in the Schism War. Most Merinita magi didn’t participate in the Schism War, but these three fought because of House Diedne’s alliance with the Munster Tuatha Dé. After the final confrontation at the Battle of Clontarf, the magi moved to Cape Clear Island, lying just off Ireland’s southwest shore.

*Cliffheart has never discovered why the Tuatha Dé allied with House Diedne. After two hundred years of queries and interviews it remains a mystery. *

*The covenant’s cathac is a Diedne magus turned into a stone obelisk that has a hand-sized hole running through it. The local monastic village calls it the Marriage Stone, and superstition has it that if a man extends his arm through the hole and grasps a woman’s hand the pair will be together forever. The monks try to prevent couples from doing this, citing the practice as pagan and evil.*

1016 Iberia (North): The Flambeau of Brigantia are captured and forced to lead the final assault against Mons Vindius. Soon, the Diedne covenant falls.

1016 Provencal: Bellaquin covenant founded.

1016–1020 Stonehenge: Cnut, younger brother of the king of Denmark conquers the whole of England.

*There was simply not enough magi left to stand against any assistance the Danes may have had from the Order of Odin, but they seem to have been kept out afterwards - or was their power waning? Perhaps magi weren’t even involved on either side?*

1017 Iberia (North). The Diedne covenant of Nebula is destroyed.

1018 Iberia: Treaty of Barcelona made between Order of Hermes and Order of Geonim, the Kabbalist magi of the Jews. The destruction of the recent Schism War fresh in mind, the magi of Barcelona make peace with the Kabbalists. They escort representatives of the Kabbalah to Tribunal and successfully negotiate a pact of non-interference between the Order and the Jews. This pact marks the first time that the Order of Hermes chose to live in peace with non-Hermetic wizards. Earlier, any such wizards would have been assimilated or destroyed.

*This is the first and only known peace between magical Orders.*

*The magic of the Kabbalists remains alien, but fortunately they seem unthreatening.*

1018 Iberian Tribunal (North). The end of the Schism War is declared. Brutus ex Flambeau is elected Praeco after the death of Ibn Sahl ex Tremere. The formation of the Iberian Chamber, an organization policing Tribunal-wide affairs, is approved. 

Veterans of the Schism War establish Durato.

1018 Normandy Tribunal (22nd): The Schism War has ended and the Quaesitors present the Tribunal with a list of magical resources seized from magi and covenants destroyed during the conflict. Fudarus, as the only surviving Breton covenant, attends the Normandy Tribunal meeting, adding Brittany to the Tribunal’s territories. Pertheus of House Tytalus successfully proposes that the aggressors have the right of conquest over these vis sources and books, but that in seven years’ time, a tournament should be held to redistribute them.

1018 Rhine Tribunal: The two remaining magi of the lineage of Hercynius renounce their membership in House Bonisagus and are accepted into House Ex Miscellanea.

*Due to their failed diplomacy and reduced numbers, House Bonisagus has never quite enjoyed the same level of influence in the Rhine Tribunal as it had before the Schism War.*

1018 Thebes: 22nd Theban Tribunal. Aegaea recognized, discussion how Redcaps to visit.

House Tremere insists that Theban magi assisted the Byzantine emperor in the invasion of Bulgaria in 1014. The claim could not be proven.

*It is true that certain magi, especially Gentle Gifted Jerbitons, had close connections with the emperor and other powerful families of the Byzantine Empire.*


## The Age of Faith and Fire (AD 1018–1160)

*As the dust of the Schism War finally settles and the Order’s wounds begin to scar over, Mythic Europe enters a turbulent era defined by both spiritual fervor and expanding horizons. The mundane world is aflame with renewed religious zeal - monastic movements spread across the continent, and new pilgrimages or "holy" conflicts (such as the First Crusade) bring fresh opportunities and dire risks to magi who must decide how (or whether) to engage.*

*Within the Order, the trauma of the Diedne purges still casts a lingering shadow. Many covenants feel torn between renewed hope in a future free of internal strife and the ever-present paranoia that another hidden corruption might bring war once again. House Tremere’s power is met with wariness; House Guernicus struggles to repair its reputation for impartial justice. Even Tytalus, ostensibly restored after its diabolism scandals, finds acceptance slow in coming.*

*Yet these decades are also a time of unprecedented growth. New covenants sprout on old battlefields or near the booming trade routes of the Mediterranean. Scholars refine Hermetic theory, building on the achievements of Bonisagus, while Mystery Cults quietly expand their initiation rites. The Dominion likewise grows mightier as churches, shrines, and holy orders flourish, forcing magi to navigate the ascending power of bishops and kings. Many soon discover that the holy zeal of crusaders can be as lethal to a magus’s secretive research as any Hermetic feud.*

*By the mid-12th century, the Order stands at a crossroads: great promise for deeper cooperation and magical discoveries set against rising worldly entanglements and still-simmering suspicions. Whether these trials galvanize a stronger, more unified Order - or push it into fresh crises - remains uncertain. What is clear is that, in the Age of Faith and Fire, few magi can ignore the conflagration of beliefs, politics, and ambition blazing through Europe’s cathedrals and courts.*

1019 Iberia (Al-Andalus): Construction begins on the so-called ‘Great Hall of Saragossa’, intended as a common place for the meetings of the Andalusian court. By 1026, the building houses the Tribunals, has a large public library, and holds the records of the new Tribunal. 

1019 Rome: The Thirteenth Verdi Competition.

1019: House Bjornaer, now led by Primus Salmo, reemerges from Crintera after 15 years. \
*Under his guidance, House Bjornaer kept a low profile, and its members gradually became more similar to the standard Hermetic magus in a deliberate attempt to prevent itself from becoming a target in the event of a future war.*

*Some claim their new Harmonist agenda is based on Hérisson’s philosophy. *

1020 Normandy: Orchard of Sour Apples covenant, vassal of Confluensis, forms after the Schism War.

*It was later in 1051 the site of the relocation of Confluensis, and its members joined its liege covenant.*

1021: Epidemics of St. Vitus’ Dance sweep Europe.

1021 Normandy: Lixivia covenant, vassal of Florum, is established in the duchy of Champagne just as the Champagne trade fairs are established - for the express purpose of acting as an intermediary between the Order and mundane merchants.

*The covenant consisted of Redcaps and Gentle Gifted magi, and altruistically offered to source any material desired by any covenant. However, magi were suspicious of their motives, and they soon became the popular prey of raiding. Driven into poverty, the covenant disbanded in 1124.*

1022 Transylvania: Tremere conference at Coeris.

1023 Thebes: Covenant Gigas established by House Tremere, initially as a place for recovery by those magi and favored companions who had been wounded in

the Schism War.

*From the very beginning the Tribunal was suspicious of its agenda, particularly when in 1069 it established a policy of admitting only members of House Tremere.*

1024 Thebes: The all-Mercere covenant of Miphoria in Smyrna changes name to Alexandria after moving across town to an abandoned fortress.

*Miphoria had relied on fantastical and overt magical means of transport, including riding a species of giant magical sea turtles. The Tribunal had decided that its members were drawing undue mundane attention to the Order, and asked them to keep a lower profile.*

1025 Iberia (Al-Andalus): The former magi of al-Zahra create the covenant that would eventually become known as Issah.

1025 Normandy Tribunal (23rd): Pertheus of Tytalus proposes a series of rulings to end disputes over magical resources in the Tribunal, later called the Perthean Compact.

The Tribunal rules that a vis source that is within a day’s return march of an established covenant of the Normandy Tribunal is considered to belong to that covenant automatically; such a source is called a seisin. A seisin recognized by the Tribunal grants its owners exclusive right of harvest.

It is ruled that a vis source which is too distant to be declared a seisin can be made a legacy of the discoverer by a vote of the Tribunal. A legacy lease issued by the Tribunal offers a covenant exclusive rights to harvest from the source, but the terms of the lease last only seven years.

Upon finding a vis source, the discoverer must report its location to a Quaesitor, who grants an interim lease on the vis until it can be officially declared at the next Tribunal meeting. 

1025 Rhine Tribunal: Arising from the aftermath of the Schism War, the Lindengilde is formed mostly by former magi of Fengheld. The purpose of this gild is to increase cooperation and trust between magi and covenants to the benefit of all, to resolve both internal and external conflicts peacefully, and to end the apathy and divisions in the Tribunal. 

*This is an altruistic and well-regarded gild, although weak; thus it is the typical go-between faction.*

1025 Thebes: 23rd Theban Tribunal. Alexandria is recognized as Miphoria renamed.

1025 Stonehenge: A Criamon sponsored covenant, Stellasper, is founded in the Scilly Isles. They remain aloof from the Tribunal.

The last Praesis Septentrionalis, the seven covenants established by Pralix in the former territory of Damhan-Allaidh to watch out for his return, fails and the watch is abandoned. 

1028 Normandy: Oleron covenant established in Aquitaine by Kherion of Merinita after defeating the previously undefeated knight Lanval, consort to the Faerie Queen of Oleron.

*He also marries Lanval’s daughter, who had become the Faerie Queen of the Court of the Apple Mile.*

1028 Provence: William of Aquitaine falls mortally ill, and a sorceress is convicted of inflicting the illness upon him.

1029 Hibernia: Since its inception the Mercere House of Leth Cuinn had been interested in martial pursuits. Things took a dramatic turn when Dáire Farranta returned after a seven year absence and challenged the leader of the covenant - who was his parens - to Wizard War. Dáire won this war without a spell being cast (since both opponents were unGifted) using remarkable feats of athleticism and swordplay that were straight out of legend - the clesrada. The remaining Redcaps were exiled, and he repopulated the covenant with his own kin.

*Among the incomers was Úathach, the faerie woman who had trained him in the clesrada. Dáire Farranta turned the Mercere House into an outpost of the Cult of Heroes, and it is still run by his descendents. Leth Cuinn trains Redcaps in Heroic*

*Virtues, and teach them the clesrada. In return for various considerations, Leth Cuinn also initiates other grogs and companions of magi into the clesrada. *

**1030 Eleventh Grand Tribunal: "The Reconciliation." This Grand Tribunal settles most of the remaining matters of the Schism War. The Tribunal ratifies the existence of the New Slavonic Tribunal and gives it formal authority over the settlement of new covenants in Slavic lands,**

**despite protests from the Rhine Tribunal.**

C. 1030 Stonehenge: Covenant Castrum Antiquum mysteriously destroyed over the winter of 1030–1031. The Redcap traveled to the site, as she had done only a year before, and found nothing but the ruins of a Roman fort. There was no trace of the covenant, not even ruins of the additional structures they had built, as if it had never existed. Rumors run rampant and a Diedne resurgence is feared, but no further events develop. 

*This remains an enduring mystery of the Order. Among the lost magi at Castrum Antiquum was Agricola, a maga who could raise gold and gems from the plants of the soil.*

1031 Iberia: The Umayyad Caliphate completely splits into taifas. 

1032 Iberia (Al-Andalus): Occelum Durii is re-established. Mons Niger is officially constituted.

1032 Normandy Tribunal: The Tribunal institutes the need for holders of a legacy lease to acquire renewal of that lease from the Tribunal every seven years at the Tribunal meeting; if the Tribunal votes against the renewal of the lease, the Tribunal takes possession of the legacy and it becomes a tropaeum instead.

*Traditionally, any newly discovered vis source is a legacy of the covenant that found it, and it is very common for a new legacy lease to be granted without serious competition. The only exception is if the vis source is close enough to another covenant to be considered a seisin; the rulings regarding seisins take precedence over legacies.*

It is also ruled that the known vis sources that are not seisins of a covenant or currently held under a legacy lease are tropaea (singular tropaeum, meaning "trophy") and owned in common by the Tribunal as a whole. Tropaea are to be

distributed among the members of the Tribunal on seven-year leases by being won in the Tourney. 

1032 Normandy: Geoffroy Martel, Count of Anjou, founds the Abbey of the Trinity after he saw three flaming spears fall from the sky and pierce the ground near a spring. He donates to the abbey the arm of St. George and a tear of Christ, shed at the tomb of Lazarus.

*It becomes one of the most powerful religious foundations in France.*

1032 Transylvania: Tremere conference at Coeris.

1033: Panic spreads throughout Europe that the end of the universe may be near, on the 1,000th anniversary of the crucifixion of Christ.

*The Book of Revelation (Chapter 20) predicts the end of the earth after a 1,000 year period after the second return of Jesus Christ, but disappointingly little seems to have happened besides some unusually harsh spring weather. *

1033 Levant: A major earthquake devastates multiple cities across Palestine region, killing many heathens.

1034 Rhine: Bellephon of Prague is devoured alive by maggots while he is being questioned by Quaesitors.

*The Disciples of the Worm have been accused repeatedly of Infernalism or practicing demonology. Somehow the cult members who were investigated have always managed to prove their innocence, or have died during the investigation.*

1037 Rome: The Fourteenth Verdi Competition.

1039 Rhine Tribunal: Fengheld covenant founded by veteran members of Houses Flambeau and Tremere returning from the Schism War in Stonehenge and Normandy.

*The Tribunal had no problems agreeing on the founding of a covenant for these "war heroes," but it was originally intended as a place that older magi could spend their glory days in isolation from mundanes. It is currently the largest covenant in the Rhine.*

1039 New Slavonic Tribunal: Grand Silesia destroyed by crusading anti-pagan knights, refugees flee to Pripet Maior.

*The Rhine Tribunal begins agitating to gain Poland shortly after. Vile tongues claim they had something to do with the destruction of the last covenant in Poland.*

1039 Normandy Tribunal: It is ruled that a vis source owned by the Tribunal that relies heavily on the abilities or skill of the harvester becomes a luctatio ("contest") rather than a tropaeum. The list of all luctationes owned by the Tribunal is a matter of public record and a copy is kept at Confluensis, although the method of obtaining the vis need not be detailed. Any magus can attempt to harvest this vis if he is prepared for the effort or danger that it poses, although succeeding in a luctatio does not confer any rights over that vis source in subsequent years. 

The patron saint of Christian magi, St Nerius is martyred at Fudarus, Domus Magnus of House Tytalus, by a collection of Tremere, Tytalus, and Flambeau wizards who eventually had succeeded in declaring a Wizard’s March against him. His Divine protection having withstood every attack, St Nerius proclaims God’s Will stronger than the petty cravings of his fellow magi, then steps from his circle of Celestial Immunity and dies.

*The records of Tribunal meetings at Fudarus never mention this event. However his life ended, St Nerius was accepted by God to be venerated as a saint.*

*The most common tale is that St Nerius was a Criamon magus born in the Provencal Tribunal. A seeker of the Enigma, St Nerius discovered it in a secret well hidden in an Alpine ravine. Instead of succumbing to the mesmerizing vision in the well, St Nerius resisted by asking the angel Michael for assistance. Bathed in the powerful Light of the Lord, St Nerius’s prayer was answered, and he emerged from the mountains a newly made saint and miracle-worker. *

*The various stories of his travels are miraculous and many magi find them hard to completely believe. It is told that through the Divine gifts he received from God, St Nerius was able to surpass many of the limits of Hermetic magic. He could cure the sick without vis, travel instantaneously from location to location, and change the essential nature of things. He never lost a Certamen match, nor could his Divine protection be pierced by spells. Some tales say that St Nerius is one of the protectors of Arcadia, and other legends tell of his incredible adventures in Purgatory.*

1039 Normandy: A new wave of ergotism strikes the Tribunal. Mortals go mad in droves.

1039 Thebes: 25th Theban Tribunal recognizes the Flambeau covenant of Kyddopetre, founded south of the Tarsus Mountains in the Cilician Gates with the stated purpose of discovering and exploring eastern Anatolia.

1040 Thebes: Magus Helios of Flambeau accused of interfering with mortals by purchasing books from them. It was found that he used no magic in these purchases, and was acquitted.

c. 1040 Loch Leglean: The Steward of Mormaer and claimant to the Caledonian throne, MacBeth, defeats and kills his rival, Duncan, the enthroned king, with the aid of Pictish wizards and renegade magi Ex Miscellanea. 

*Only one of many cases of magical meddling in Loch Leglean.*

1042 Transylvania: Tremere conference at Coeris.

*We need more information about these…*

1046 Iberian Tribunal: Tertia is abandoned.

1046 Normandy Tribunal: The claim is made by enemies of the covenant of Montverte that they are acting as court wizards by residing within the home of a mundane lord. They argue in their defense that they hold no oath of vassalage, and that they leave him to get on with running mundane matters. While their interference in the latter is clearly proven, the precedent is set based on Iola of Guernicus’ distinction - under pressure from Verditius magi who argue that if an economic relationship was deemed inadmissible, all sales of magical items would become suspect (which a few decades later it became though) - that Court wizards are generally defined as "employees," but employee here is not a function of economic support, but rather a relationship of subordination enforced by vows (usually of vassalage). It is agreed that magi have a right to defend themselves and their covenant, and its physical location is irrelevant. The Tribunal rules that, in accordance with the "not bringing ruin upon my sodales" clause, providing no danger to the Order is brought about by their actions, the situation of a covenant within a castle or manor is legal. Magical support may be offered to the companion lord as a consors - that is, as an acknowledged asset of the covenant. 

*However, in 1088 a ruling was made against Florum, who by their actions were*

*said to be upsetting the local economy. *

1046 Thebes: The 26th Theban Tribunal gathering attacked by stringles witches from Polyaigos.

1047: Himinis the Mad dies.

*May his many cursed items go unfound!*

1048 Normandy: Vexatores covenant, vassal of Fudarus, is founded - specifically to provoke Sinapis during the conflict between Houses Flambeau and Tytalus - exactly 21 miles north of the Flambeau covenant (the minimum legal distance), at the site of one of the vis sources claimed from House Diedne.

*Vexatores was not intended to be a long-term prospect, and it was abandoned when the conflict between the Houses ended in 1064.*

1048 Loch Leglean: Rumors of Damhan-Allaidh’s return plague Loch Leglean.

*Again...*

1048 Rhine: The maga Aschlaranda, formerly of Waldherz, suddenly reappeared at Durenmar with her apprentice Schadrit.

*She was accepted back into House Bonisagus, but her actions and opinions were contrary to the prevailing spirit of reconciliation, and she refused to tell of the fate of Waldherz or to renounce the Rowan Gild. After a major dispute,*

*she was (with reluctance) eventually declared orbus (see 1052). They*

*were pursued into the forest by a leading magus of the Ash Gild, Ganelon, who declared Wizard War on them both. He was never heard from again.*

1048 Rome: Fortunata of House Jerbiton, covenant Cupressa Nigra is acquitted of charges of diabolism.

1050 Rome: Covenant of Rellantalli gains land as fief from Pisa.

1051 Normandy: Dragon’s Rest covenant, vassal of Confluensis, is founded when Confluensis relocates to the Cotentin peninsula and the Orchard of Sour Apples.

1052 Rhine: The covenant of Dankmar established by Aschlaranda, a maga exiled from Durenmar and House Bonisagus, and her apprentice Schadrit.

*Her intent was to oppose that great covenant at every turn, and she named it Dankmar in parody of the birthplace of the Order. Now the ancient Schadrit of Ex Miscellanea squats in the center of this Winter covenant with brooding malice, while descendants act according to his will.*

1052 New Slavonic Tribunal: Casimir had driven the Czechs out of Poland, and slavic magi could come back to Poland. Leczcya covenant is founded by some of the survivors of Grand Silesia, reinforced by the ruling of the Grand Tribunal of 1030 that the Slavonic Tribunal was granted formal authority over settlements in all Slavic lands - to the ire of the Rhine Tribunal.

*It is from this time that the strong links between House Bjornaer and Pripet Maior date. The magi of Grand Silesia had twice been forced to flee there, and now many of them chose to stay.*

1052 Transylvania: Tremere conference at Coeris.

1053 Rhine Tribunal: The most contested and controversial part of the Rhine’s Peripheral Code is established; that the lifeblood of the Tribunal lies within its forests, as they are the wellsprings of magic and magi have a duty to preserve and protect them. "We must become Guardians of the Forests, for none will halt this decline if we do not."

Under pressure from House Guernicus, the following clause was later added:

"We must take care to only act in such a fashion so as not to incur the wrath of the mundanes, else the Oath of Hermes will be broken, and due punishment meted forthwith."

*This Peripheral Code addendum is problematic as it has no inherent method of enforcement, and may be interpreted in many different ways. Dankmar was not punished for aiding the destruction of a new monastery in the Black Forest, yet Crintera was refused permission to use this ruling to halt the Danish invasion of Rügen. Decisions regarding the "Guardians of the Forests" ruling seem to depend entirely upon political maneuvering and the opinions of the presiding Quaesitor.*

*Dankmar is occupied by Aschlaranda and her apprentice Schadrit, but is not recognized by the Tribunal due to Durenmar’s inﬂuence.*

1053 Thebes: 27th Theban Tribunal again raided by stringles witches from the island of Polyaigos.

*When it was discovered that a renegade Flambeau magus was hiding in their midst, aiding their depravities, the People’s Assembly of the Tribunal called for action.*

1053 New Slavonic Tribunal: The growth of Three Lakes, and its ever increasing involvement in Novgorod city, was drawing the political center of the Tribunal northward. The Tribunal changes its name to Novgorod Tribunal.
